## 🔴 P1 FIXED 2026-07-05 — `/api/market/gex-heatmap`'s `shift`/`vex_shift` served a stale, present-tense "migration just happened" summary on a closed market (task #174, branch `fix/gex-shift-stale-off-hours`)

**Status:** FIXED. Flagged by an external audit (a separate Cursor session on this repo): "SPX heatmap returns `shift.available: true`, summary 'Over the last 2h14m: gamma flip migrated...' on a closed market." Independently re-verified this session by reading the live source before applying any fix.

**Root cause (`src/lib/providers/polygon-options-gex.ts:2284-2320`):** `shift`/`vex_shift` are computed by `computeGexShift`/`computeVexShift` (thin delegates over the generic `computeMetricShift`, `polygon-options-gex.ts:1592-1666`), which diff the just-appended current GEX/VEX snapshot against the earliest still-in-window history snapshot and produce a present-tense summary string — `"Over the last {elapsed}: the {N} call wall built +X%, gamma flip migrated up Y pts ({read})."` — via `fmtElapsed(since_ms)`. Per the block's own comment (`polygon-options-gex.ts:2284`): `// ── SHIFT (intraday migration) — fresh compute ONLY ── ... Computed once here and cached with the matrix (all users read the cached shift — never per user).` **This computation has ZERO market-hours awareness anywhere in the path** — it runs unconditionally whenever the shared matrix cache refreshes, with no gate on whether the market is open at either the moment it's computed OR the moment it's later read from cache. Once a refresh happens to land during RTH, the cached `shift` object — present-tense summary and all — is served UNCHANGED to every subsequent poll from every user through the ENTIRE closed period (evening, overnight, weekend, holiday) until the next refresh, reading as if the migration just happened when the market has actually been closed the whole time.

**Consumer contract confirming this is user-visible, not just an internal artifact:** `src/components/desk/GexHeatmap.tsx:2587-2592` reads `data.shift`/`data.vex_shift` per the active lens and derives `hasShiftForLens`/`shift.available` purely as booleans; the Shift panel (`GexHeatmap.tsx:3351-3374`) renders the real `ShiftView` — including the live summary sentence — whenever `hasShiftForLens && shift && shift.available`, per its own comment: "no shift → the Shift tab is hidden/disabled for them (never fabricated)." The component never independently checks whether the underlying data is stale relative to wall-clock now — it trusts the server's `available` flag completely, so the server serving a stale-but-technically-`available:true` object is sufficient to mislead a real user into believing a migration is actively happening.

**Why it wasn't caught earlier:** this is the same "stale content served as live" bug class already fixed twice this session — `/api/market/regime` (task #173, PR #523: a Friday holiday capture still `available: true` ~49h later on Sunday) and, before that, `/api/platform/intel`'s `lastBrief` via `src/lib/providers/spx-session.ts`'s `isPremarketBriefFresh` gate. Each of those hardening passes fixed its own endpoint but didn't sweep every "cache a computed-at-refresh-time object and serve it as-is" code path in the codebase — this one, sitting one layer deeper (inside `polygon-options-gex.ts`'s shared matrix build, several call sites removed from the `market_regime` table pattern), was missed until this audit.

**Evidence:** live capture per the audit — `shift.available: true` with a fresh-reading `"Over the last 2h14m: gamma flip migrated..."` summary returned while the market was closed. Reproduced locally via the new route test (`route.test.ts`, "off-hours shift gate" suite): mocked `fetchGexHeatmap` to return a heatmap whose `shift`/`vex_shift` carry a real `computeMetricShift`-shaped payload (`available: true`, populated `delta_by_strike`/`flip_migration`/`wall_changes`/`summary`) and mocked `isEtCashRth()` to `false` — pre-fix, the route would pass that object straight through (`available: true`, live summary intact); post-fix, the response overrides both to `{ available: false, status: "collecting" }` with `summary`/`delta_by_strike`/`flip_migration` all `undefined`. The same fixture with `isEtCashRth()` mocked `true` passes through unchanged, proving the happy path (during RTH) has no regression.

**Blast radius:** both `shift` (GEX, always present) and `vex_shift` (VEX, optional/additive) go through the identical `computeMetricShift` engine and hit the identical route-level spread (`route.ts`'s `roundFloats({ ...heatmap, ... })`) — so both needed the same override; the fix applies to both, and a dedicated test (`"vex_shift.available is ALSO forced false..."`) confirms it wasn't just fixed for the GEX case that the audit happened to quote. No other endpoint reads `polygon-options-gex.ts`'s shift/vex_shift fields (grepped: `gex-heatmap/route.ts` and `gex-heatmap/explain/route.ts` — the latter doesn't touch shift at all, confirmed by reading it, so out of scope).

**Fix rationale — why the route, not `polygon-options-gex.ts`:** the override lives in `src/app/api/market/gex-heatmap/route.ts` (after the `roundFloats(...)` call, ~line 342), not inside `computeMetricShift`/the cache-refresh path. "Is the market closed RIGHT NOW" is a property of THIS READ (the moment a user's poll hits the route), not of the moment the cache was last (re)computed — the route already holds the freshest wall-clock read via `isEtCashRth()` (`src/lib/et-market-hours.ts`, the canonical cash-RTH gate already "used by UI polling, options WS, correctness, and cron health" per its own doc comment), and critically, EVERY request during the closed window reads the SAME cached object — gating at compute time would only suppress the object on the ONE refresh that happens to straddle the close, not the hundreds of poll requests that read the stale cache afterward. Overriding to `false` REGARDLESS of what the cached computation produced — rather than trying to infer staleness from `since_ms`/`baseline_ts` — was chosen because it's the simplest correct statement of the actual rule ("nothing computed while markets are closed should ever read as live") and mirrors the codebase's own existing `shift.available:false → hide it, never fabricate` contract exactly, just extending "nothing to show" to include "the thing we'd show is real but stale-right-now." When overriding, the ENTIRE object is replaced with the same minimal `{ available: false, status: "collecting" }` shape the cold-start ("not enough history yet") path already uses — not just the boolean — so the misleading present-tense `summary` string (the actual audit-quoted artifact) never leaves the server while the market is closed; leaving `available: false` next to an intact live-reading `summary` would only be a half-fix for any raw-JSON consumer or future UI that doesn't gate on `available` the same way today's `GexHeatmap.tsx` does. Reused the existing `"collecting"` status literal rather than inventing a new one (e.g. `"closed"`) after confirming (grep) that no consumer branches on `status`'s value — only on `available`'s truthiness — so a new literal would add type-surface with zero behavioral effect; the smaller diff was preferred. Deliberately left untouched, per the task's explicit scope: `computeMetricShift`'s core diff math, the wall/flip-migration calculations, and the cache-refresh trigger logic — all correct, none related to this presentation-layer bug.

**Client-side check (no change needed):** verified `GexHeatmap.tsx`'s Shift panel (`~3351-3374`) derives `shift`/`hasShiftForLens` fresh from `data` (the SWR poll result) on every render — there is no local component state buffering a "shift was available last time" flag, and the panel is not a separately-toggled tab (it's a section within the "pair-b" `Tabs` view, `~3986-3997`) that could get stuck showing a previously-rendered `ShiftView`. So the next poll that returns `available: false` immediately flips the render to the existing "Building positioning history" `EmptyState` — no broken/blank panel, no client change required.

**Tests added (`src/app/api/market/gex-heatmap/route.test.ts`, extended — new `describe("off-hours shift gate")` block, 5 tests):**
- Outside RTH, `shift.available` is forced `false` even though the mocked heatmap carries a REAL computed shift (summary/deltas/flip_migration all populated) — and the whole object is blanked, not just the flag.
- Outside RTH, `vex_shift.available` is independently forced `false` too (proving the GEX-only quote in the audit isn't the only affected field).
- Outside RTH, a heatmap with no `vex_shift` field at all stays without one — the gate never fabricates a block that wasn't present.
- DURING RTH, the identical real shift/vex_shift fixtures pass through completely unchanged (regression guard on the happy path).
- Outside RTH, an already-`{available:false, status:"collecting"}` cold-start shift stays exactly that (the gate is a no-op on the pre-existing "no history yet" case, not a second code path that could diverge from it).
All 4 pre-existing tests in this file continue to pass unmodified (the new `isEtCashRth` mock defaults to "market open," making the gate a no-op for every test that doesn't explicitly flip it).

**Verification:** `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (this task never touches SPX Slayer). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite (`/opt/node22/bin/node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`, Node 22.22.2): **1629/1629 passing, 0 failures** (71 suites; 5 net-new in this file). `npm run build` clean.

---
## 🔴 P1 FIXED 2026-07-05 — `/api/market/gex-heatmap` reported `available: true` on an empty/zero-spot heatmap — a "usable" flag on data with nothing real to show (task #172, branch `fix/gex-heatmap-false-available`)

**Status:** FIXED. Surfaced by an external audit (a parallel Cursor session on this repo) and independently re-verified this session by reading the live source.

**Root cause (`src/app/api/market/gex-heatmap/route.ts:271-327`, pre-fix):** `fetchGexHeatmap()` (`src/lib/providers/polygon-options-gex.ts`) can return a **non-null** `GexHeatmap` via its internal `emptyHeatmap()` fallback (~line 2422) at two call sites in `buildGexHeatmapUncached`: (1) line ~1975, when `resolveSpotSnapshot` fails to resolve a spot at all (`spot: 0`, `strikes: []`), and (2) line ~1998, when the spot resolves but the options-chain band fetch returns zero contracts (real `spot`, `strikes: []`). Both produce a real, non-null object — never `null` — with `gex.regime.read: "No options-chain data for this ticker — dealer gamma profile unavailable."`. The route's `if (!heatmap)` guard (line 273) only catches the true-null case (Polygon fully unconfigured / no options root resolvable); it does not catch either `emptyHeatmap()` shape. Falling through, the route unconditionally set `available: true` at the top of the `roundFloats({...})` call (line 317), regardless of what the heatmap object actually contained — so a thin/dead/unresolved ticker reported itself as usable.

**Why it wasn't caught earlier:** the `!heatmap` null-check reads as "no data, tell the client," which is correct for the null case — but `emptyHeatmap()`'s entire design intent (a valid, non-throwing placeholder object instead of `null`, per its own doc comment) means "no usable data" and "a real, empty object" are two different runtime shapes that both need the same `available:false` treatment, and only one of them was wired to it. No test exercised the route's `available` computation against a mocked empty/zero-spot heatmap — the route had zero test coverage before this fix (`route.test.ts` is new).

**Evidence:** confirmed live shape for SPY/QQQ per the external audit: `{ "available": true, "spot": 0, "strikes": [], "gex": { "regime": { "read": "No options-chain data for this ticker — dealer gamma profile unavailable." } } }`. Reproduced locally: mocked `fetchGexHeatmap` to return `emptyHeatmap()`'s exact shape (spot:0, strikes:[]) and confirmed the route's JSON response had `available: true` pre-fix; reverted to pre-fix code (`git stash`) and re-ran the new test suite — the two "unusable heatmap" tests fail cleanly (`true !== false`) against the old code while the happy-path and null-heatmap tests still pass, proving the tests exercise the actual gap, not just the fix's own logic. Restored the fix and re-ran — all 4 pass.

**Blast radius:** this route's own `available` flag is read by (1) `src/components/desk/GexHeatmap.tsx` — the public Heat Maps UI, which drives its `live`/`quoteOnly`/`Offline` header badge off `Boolean(data?.available)`; (2) `validate-live-prod`/`live-fixes-audit`-style external validators, which the reporting audit flagged as false-passing thin-chain tickers because `available:true` reads as "usable" without also checking `strikes.length`. Checked the consumer component directly: `GexHeatmap.tsx`'s main-content `empty` gate (line ~2726) is `!isLoading && data != null && (!data.available || strikes.length === 0)` — it ALREADY treats `strikes.length === 0` as empty independent of the `available` flag, so the component was never at risk of crashing or rendering a broken matrix on the pre-fix payload; it renders its existing "No options chain" `EmptyState`. The header's `quoteOnly` badge (`Boolean(data?.available) && !hasStrikes`, line ~2515) was written to distinguish "spot resolved, chain thin" (emptyHeatmap call site #2 above, real spot) from a hard "Offline" — post-fix, since `available` is now `false` whenever `strikes.length === 0` (matching this task's explicit contract), that distinction is no longer reachable from the server and the header will show "Offline" instead of "Quote only" for a real-spot/empty-chain ticker. Deliberately left as-is: no crash, the main content area's behavior is unchanged (already handled both flags), and re-introducing a 3-way client-side distinction was out of scope for what is intentionally a single-flag route fix — flagged here for visibility, not fixed, since CLAUDE.md scopes one issue per branch/PR and the task instructions were explicit that this should be surgical to the route.

**Fix:** replaced the hardcoded `available: true` with a computed `heatmapUsable = heatmap.spot > 0 && heatmap.strikes.length > 0`, mirroring the exact same "no usable data" semantics the pre-existing `!heatmap` branch already used (that branch's `available: false` fires when there's no object at all; this makes a non-null-but-empty object report the same `false`). Did not touch `emptyHeatmap()` itself or `fetchGexHeatmap`'s null-vs-object contract (out of scope per the task) — this is purely the route correctly reporting availability for the object it already receives. The full (empty-valued) heatmap object is still spread into the response (`...heatmap`) exactly as before — only the `available` field's value changed — so no other field/shape in the payload moved.

**Tests added (new file, `src/app/api/market/gex-heatmap/route.test.ts` — this route had zero test coverage before):**
- A non-null but empty heatmap (`spot:0, strikes:[]`, the true SPY/QQQ-observed shape) now reports `available:false`.
- A resolved-spot/empty-chain heatmap (`spot:452.1, strikes:[]`, the other `emptyHeatmap()` call site) also reports `available:false` — same "nothing to show" outcome regardless of which upstream failure produced the empty object.
- A real, non-empty heatmap still reports `available:true` (no regression on the happy path).
- A true `null` heatmap still short-circuits to the pre-existing minimal `{ available:false, underlying }` shape (unchanged).

**Verification:** `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (this PR never touches SPX Slayer). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite (`/opt/node22/bin/node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`, Node 22.22.2): **1612/1612 passing, 0 failures** (69 suites, 4 net-new). `npm run build` clean.

---

## 🟠 FIXED 2026-07-05 — `/api/market/regime` GET served days-old regime snapshots as `available: true` with no staleness signal, AND leaked raw unrounded NUMERIC strings for `netGex`/`ivPercentile` (task #173, branch `fix/market-regime-staleness-netgex-round`)

**Status:** FIXED. Found by an external Cursor audit session, independently re-verified this session by reading the live route source (`src/app/api/market/regime/route.ts`) before any fix was applied.

**Bug A — no staleness signal. Severity: P1.** `GET` always ran `SELECT * FROM market_regime ORDER BY captured_at DESC LIMIT 1` and returned `available: true` for whatever that row was — `capturedAt` was present in the payload, but nothing told the client whether it was live or ancient. **Evidence (the audit's live capture):** `capturedAt: "2026-07-03T15:45:35.231Z"` — a Friday-afternoon snapshot — was still being served `available: true`, full `playbook` text included, on Sunday 2026-07-05, ~49 hours later. Notably, 2026-07-03 is itself the July-4th-observed market holiday (`src/lib/nighthawk/session.ts`'s `US_MARKET_HOLIDAYS`) — the capture predates the `isSpxEngineCronWindow` holiday-gate fix (`src/lib/spx-play-session-guards.ts:78-86`, confirmed live the same day per that file's own comment), so this exact row is a fossil of that earlier bug as well as an instance of this one. **Root cause (`route.ts:17-39`, pre-fix):** the "last row wins" query has no concept of session boundaries — `market-regime-detector` (`src/app/api/cron/market-regime-detector/route.ts`) only writes during `isSpxEngineCronWindow` (weekday trading-day RTH, every 5 min), so on any healthy trading day the top row is fresh, but nights/weekends/holidays/cron-outages all serve the last successful write with no signal it isn't current. **Why it wasn't caught earlier:** this route predates the "stale content served as live" bug class this codebase already hardened elsewhere (`isPremarketBriefFresh` for `platform_briefs`/`/api/brief/premarket`, task-documented in `src/lib/providers/spx-session.ts` after a near-identical live incident on `/api/platform/intel`'s `lastBrief`) — that hardening pass evidently didn't reach this sibling "last one wins" endpoint.

**Bug B — `netGex`/`ivPercentile` unrounded raw strings. Severity: P2.** `net_gex` and `iv_percentile` are Postgres `NUMERIC` columns (`src/lib/migrations/004_god_tier_features.sql:11-12`); node-postgres never parses `NUMERIC` into a JS `number` (avoids silent precision loss on the driver side) — it returns a full-precision **string**. Both were serialized verbatim into the response instead of going through this codebase's `roundFloats` convention (`src/lib/round-floats.ts`, already used by `gex-heatmap`/`gex-positioning` for the identical "raw postgres/float noise leaks to clients" class documented in this file's history and in `CLAUDE.md`'s "Data-correctness notes learned"). **Evidence:** the audit's live capture had `netGex` as `"7730543991.5...93"` — a string, not a number, with 13 fractional digits. `iv_percentile` shares the exact same column type and exposure pattern in this same route but wasn't separately called out by the audit (likely because the sampled row's percentile happened to round cleanly) — found during blast-radius review of this fix and fixed alongside `netGex` rather than left for a second pass, since it's the identical root cause in the identical response.

**Fix:**
- `stale: boolean` — computed by comparing `captured_at`'s ET calendar date (`formatEtDate`) against `mostRecentTradingDayEt(now)`, a new helper added to `src/lib/nighthawk/session.ts` (mirrors the existing `nextTradingDayEt`'s forward walk, just backward — reuses the same `isTradingDayEt`/`formatEtDate` primitives rather than hand-rolling a new date calc, per CLAUDE.md's "prefer reusing a helper" convention). An unparseable/missing `captured_at` fails CLOSED (`stale: true`) rather than silently claiming freshness. New unit tests in `src/lib/nighthawk/session.test.ts` cover: same-day passthrough, walking back across the July-4th-observed holiday weekend (Sun 07-05 → Thu 07-02, mirroring the exact live scenario above), and a plain weekend.
- `marketOpen: boolean` — the existing canonical cash-RTH gate, `isEtCashRth()` (`src/lib/et-market-hours.ts`, already "used by UI polling, options WS, correctness, and cron health" per its own doc comment) — reused as-is, not duplicated.
- **Contract decision — `available` stays `true`, `stale`/`marketOpen` are purely additive:** the task brief's default assumption was that `src/lib/largo/run-tool.ts`'s `get_market_regime` Largo tool is this route's "one known consumer." That's false — read the actual case body (`run-tool.ts:951-954`): `get_market_regime` calls `fetchPlatformIntelSnapshot()` (`src/lib/nighthawk/platform-intel-snapshot.ts`), a completely different data source that never touches `/api/market/regime`. A full-repo grep (`grep -rn "market/regime"` across `src/`, excluding this route's own file and its self-referential `console.error` string) found **zero** internal fetch call sites for this GET endpoint — no frontend component, no Largo tool, nothing. This route is a standalone public JSON endpoint with no known internal consumer to potentially break. Given that, flipping `available: false` off-hours would be an unforced, unverifiable behavior change; adding `stale`/`marketOpen` as new fields alongside the unchanged existing ones is strictly non-breaking for any consumer (internal, not-yet-built, or external) that already exists or gets added later.
- `netGex`/`ivPercentile`: coerced via a small `numericOrNull()` helper (`Number(raw)`, preserving SQL `NULL` as JSON `null` — never a false `0`) then passed through `roundFloats()`, matching the `gex-heatmap` convention exactly.

**Blast radius:** confined to `src/app/api/market/regime/route.ts` (GET only — POST/`isCronAuthorized` untouched, confirmed by an unmodified `git diff` on those lines) and the new `mostRecentTradingDayEt` helper in `src/lib/nighthawk/session.ts` (additive export, zero changes to any existing function in that file). `iv_percentile` was the one same-root-cause sibling found and fixed alongside `net_gex`, per the fix note above. `flow_ratio` (also `NUMERIC`) is written by the cron but never read back out in this GET response, so it's out of this route's exposure surface — not touched.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Node 22.22.2 (`node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`): **1619/1619 passing, 0 failures** (69 suites; 3 new in `session.test.ts`, 8 new in the new `src/app/api/market/regime/route.test.ts` — fresh-row/not-stale, multi-day-old-row/stale, missing-timestamp fails-closed, `netGex`/`ivPercentile` real-number rounding, null-stays-null, and the pre-existing no-rows/DB-error `available:false` paths regression-guarded). Node 20.20.2 (identical invocation): **1619/1619 passing, 0 failures** — identical counts, no Node-20-vs-22 divergence. `npm run build` clean (`✓ Compiled successfully`). `npm run lint:brand` / `npm run lint:vendor` both clean. `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (this task never touches SPX Slayer).
---

## 🟡 FIXED 2026-07-05 — `/api/account/positions/stream` (Night's Watch SSE) had NEITHER a connection cap NOR a backpressure check, unlike its 3 SSE siblings — single-session slow-loris/fan-out DoS (task #170, branch `fix/positions-stream-sse-dos-cap`)

**Status:** FIXED. Found during a security audit of this codebase's SSE surface: there are exactly 4 SSE routes (`src/app/api/market/flows/stream/route.ts`, `src/app/api/market/spx/pulse/stream/route.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/apis/stream/route.ts`, `src/app/api/account/positions/stream/route.ts`). The first 3 already carry (1) a per-instance `activeStreams >= MAX_STREAMS` cap returning 503 once hit, and (2) an `sseBackpressureExceeded(controller.desiredSize)` check before every `enqueue` that drops a stalled-read-side client instead of letting the internal queue grow unbounded — `admin/apis/stream/route.ts` even carries its own inline comment noting this exact bug class was already found and fixed there once (2637517, "the route previously had NO cap"). `account/positions/stream/route.ts` was the one sibling never brought up to the same standard.

**Severity:** MEDIUM. Not remotely exploitable pre-auth (route sits behind `requireTierApi("premium")` + a launch-flag gate, `requireToolApi("nighthawk")`, currently locked to admins only until the tool ships) — so today's blast radius is admin-only. But the gate is auth-tier, not identity- or rate-limited, and the fix must be correct BEFORE this tool launches to real premium members, at which point the gap becomes a real single-session DoS. Logged as medium rather than high given the current launch-gate mitigation, matching this doc's severity convention of rating the CURRENT exposure, not the hypothetical post-launch one — but fixed now regardless since CLAUDE.md's standing policy is fix-on-sight, not defer-until-launch.

**Root cause (`src/app/api/account/positions/stream/route.ts:20-92`, pre-fix):** `GET` had no `activeStreams`/`MAX_STREAMS` guard at all — every call unconditionally proceeded to `new ReadableStream(...)`, which spawns its own `setInterval` heartbeat (15s) plus a self-rescheduling `setTimeout` push loop (3s) that calls `getEnrichedOpenAndRecentClosedForUser(userId)` and `controller.enqueue()`s the result forever, with no upper bound on how many of these loops could exist concurrently for one instance. Separately, `send()`'s `enqueue` call was wrapped in a bare `try { controller.enqueue(...) } catch { cleanup(); }` with no `controller.desiredSize` check beforehand — so a client that opens the connection and never reads the response body (the exact "slow-loris" pattern) has its per-connection queue grow without limit instead of being disconnected. Both gaps are the identical bug class the other 3 SSE routes already close.

**Why it wasn't caught earlier:** this route predates the backpressure/cap hardening pass that touched the other 3 SSE routes (`sse-backpressure.ts` + the `admin/apis/stream` cap fix, commit 2637517) — that pass evidently didn't do a full sweep of every SSE route in the codebase, just the ones it was already looking at. No test or lint rule enforces "every `new ReadableStream` in a route handler must have a cap + backpressure check," so the omission had no mechanical trip-wire.

**Evidence:** confirmed by direct code diff against the 3 siblings — `flows/stream/route.ts` (lines 14-15, 29-31, 57-65), `spx/pulse/stream/route.ts` (lines 72-73, 79-81, 109-116), `admin/apis/stream/route.ts` (lines 16-17, 23-25, 57-65) all have the module-level `let activeStreams = 0; const MAX_STREAMS = Number(process.env.*_MAX_STREAMS ?? N);` guard plus the `sseBackpressureExceeded()` call inside `send()`; `account/positions/stream/route.ts` had neither, verified via `grep -n "activeStreams\|MAX_STREAMS\|sseBackpressureExceeded" src/app/api/account/positions/stream/route.ts` returning zero hits pre-fix. Manually reverted the fix locally and re-ran the new test suite: both new tests fail cleanly in under 2s against the pre-fix code (`200 !== 503` on the cap test; `backpressure should close the stream, not let it run forever: false !== true` on the backpressure test) — proving the tests actually exercise the gap, not just the fix's own logic.

**Blast radius:** single file/route — `account/positions/stream/route.ts` is the only remaining SSE route without this pattern; the other 3 were already fixed (2 originally, 1 via the earlier admin fix). No shared helper needed duplicating since `sseBackpressureExceeded()` (`src/lib/sse-backpressure.ts`) is already a pure, alias-free, unit-tested predicate reused as-is.

**Fix:** ported the exact pattern from `flows/stream/route.ts` verbatim: a module-level `let activeStreams = 0; const MAX_STREAMS = Number(process.env.POSITIONS_SSE_MAX_STREAMS ?? 500);` (new dedicated env var so it doesn't collide with the market routes' shared `SSE_MAX_STREAMS`), a `503` short-circuit at the top of `GET` once the cap is hit, a `counted` flag alongside the existing `closed` flag so `cleanup()` decrements `activeStreams` exactly once (idempotent, mirroring the siblings), and an `sseBackpressureExceeded(controller.desiredSize)` check inside `send()` before every `enqueue`, closing the controller and running `cleanup()` on trip. Chose `500` (matching `flows/stream`, the other user-facing/non-admin SSE route) rather than `admin/apis/stream`'s lower `100` (admins are few) or `spx/pulse/stream`'s raised `2000` (that route's higher cap was earned by a separate fan-out consolidation that doesn't apply here — this route is deliberately per-connection/per-user with no shared cache to fan out from, per its own module doc). Deliberately left the existing abort-based cleanup wiring (`req.signal.addEventListener("abort", ...)`) untouched — it already had correct idempotent teardown, it just wasn't counted against a cap.

**Tests added (new file, `src/app/api/account/positions/stream/route.test.ts` — this route had zero test coverage before):**
- "rejects a new connection with 503 once MAX_STREAMS (2) are already active, and un-caps on disconnect" — sets `POSITIONS_SSE_MAX_STREAMS=2`, opens 2 real connections (never reading their bodies), proves a 3rd is rejected with 503 while both are open, then aborts both and proves a 4th succeeds — verifying the counter is a live gauge, not a one-way trip.
- "a stalled client that never reads trips backpressure and the stream closes instead of growing unbounded" — lowers `SSE_MAX_QUEUED_CHUNKS=1` (the threshold is read per-call, not cached at module load — see `sse-backpressure.ts`), uses `node:test`'s `mock.timers` to advance the 3s push loop 8 times without ever draining the stream, and proves the stream reaches `done: true` (closed) with only 1-2 buffered chunks rather than continuing to grow — i.e. the queue is bounded regardless of how many more ticks would otherwise have occurred.
- Every opened connection is aborted in a `finally` block (even ones expected to be rejected) — a regression that silently lets the cap or backpressure check no-op would otherwise leave real, un-mocked heartbeat/push timers running forever and hang the whole test file instead of failing fast; confirmed this works by manually reverting the fix and observing clean, fast (<2s) assertion failures rather than a hang.

**Verification:** `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (this task never touches SPX Slayer). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite passing on both Node 22 and Node 20 (`--experimental-test-module-mocks`), 2 new tests. `npm run build` clean. `lint:brand` / `lint:vendor` clean.

---

## 🟡 FIXED 2026-07-05 — Nightly Largo grounding audit (`verifyLargo()`) had ZERO coverage of BIE-router-composed answers, only Claude-tool-calling answers (task #166, branch `fix/bie-persist-router-tool-results`)

**Where:** `src/lib/largo/largo-store.ts:150-159`, `fetchRecentLargoAnswersWithResults()` — the cron-readable reader `largo-verifier.ts`'s numeric-grounding engine (`verifyLargo()`) samples from. It filters `WHERE role = 'assistant' AND tool_results IS NOT NULL`. That filter is correct as written; the bug is upstream, in what gets written. `src/lib/largo-terminal.ts`'s two BIE-router call sites (`runLargoQuery` line ~318, `runLargoQueryStream` line ~443) called `appendLargoMessage(sid, userId, "assistant", routed.answer, ["blackout_intelligence"])` — no `toolResults` (6th) argument at all — for every router-composed answer (0DTE plays, SPX structure, market context; `src/lib/bie/composers.ts`). `appendLargoMessage()`'s existing (unchanged) serialization is `toolResults && toolResults.length ? JSON.stringify(toolResults) : null` (`largo-store.ts:206`), so an omitted argument always persists `tool_results = NULL`, and every router turn was silently excluded from `fetchRecentLargoAnswersWithResults()`.

**Root cause, and why it wasn't caught earlier:** this was explicit prior design, not an oversight — task #166's own predecessor fix (the "Largo's numeric-grounding verifier had no real data to run against" entry above, 2026-07-04) states outright: *"The BIE-router fast path (no tool calls, `routed.context` instead) is intentionally untouched"* and the doc comment it left on `fetchRecentLargoAnswersWithResults` said router answers "are correctly excluded rather than treated as 'zero tools used.'" That reasoning conflated two different things: the router never calls a Largo *tool* (true — `tools_used` is legitimately `["blackout_intelligence"]`, a sentinel, not a real tool name), but `composeBieAnswer()` (`composeSpxStructure`/`composeMarketContext`/`composeZeroDtePlays` in `composers.ts`) still reads real, live platform state (`routed.context`) to build a deterministic answer — that payload IS verifiable ground truth, and was simply never persisted. The practical effect: a regression in any composer that fed stale or mis-mapped numbers into an answer had **no independent after-the-fact detector** — `verifyLargo()`'s self-test (a hardcoded fixture) would keep passing forever regardless of real router-answer quality, and the "real-data check" section only ever saw Claude-tool-loop turns. Confirmed via a completed audit read of `largo-store.ts:142-159` plus both call sites in `largo-terminal.ts` — not a hypothetical, the router path (0DTE plays / SPX structure / market context, a meaningful fraction of real Largo traffic per `bieIntentBucket()`'s own cohort logic in `bie/calibration.ts`) had contributed exactly zero rows to every `verifyLargo()` run since the grounding-audit machinery shipped.

**Fix:** `routed.context` already exists at both call sites (returned from `tryBieRoute()` alongside `route`/`answer`) — pass it through as the `toolResults` argument, wrapped in a single-element array (`[routed.context]`) to match the column's "array of per-call results" shape the Claude-tool-loop path already writes (`capturedResults`, an array of individual tool outputs). `collectContextNumbers()` (`bie/verifier.ts`) recurses through arrays and plain objects identically, so wrapping a single object in a 1-element array changes nothing about which numbers are considered "grounded" — it only makes the same payload durable. Both call sites (`runLargoQuery` and `runLargoQueryStream` — duplicated logic, same root cause, both fixed) now write:
```
await appendLargoMessage(sid, userId, "assistant", routed.answer, ["blackout_intelligence"], [routed.context]);
```
Updated the now-stale doc comments on `fetchRecentLargoAnswersWithResults` and `appendLargoMessage` in `largo-store.ts` that described the old (intentional) exclusion, so a future reader doesn't reintroduce it believing it's still correct.

**Blast radius:** confined to the two call sites in `largo-terminal.ts` and the two doc comments in `largo-store.ts` — grepped every read site of `tool_results`/`toolResults`/`tools_used` on Largo messages before changing the write path. `tool_results` (the `largo_messages` column touched here) is read in exactly two other places: `largo-verifier.ts` (the intended beneficiary of this fix) and `largo-store.test.ts`. Confirmed **not** touched: `bie_interactions.tools_used` — the column every calibration-cohort function in `src/lib/bie/calibration.ts` (HELIX/Thermal/Night-Hawk/0DTE/SPX tool-calling cohorts, tasks #112/#133/#137/#144/#149) reads via `db.ts`'s `fetch*ToolCallingBieInteractions()` queries — is a completely separate table, written by `logBie()`/`insertBieInteraction()`, untouched by this change; those cohort functions already correctly treat the router's `["blackout_intelligence"]` sentinel as non-matching real-tool-name membership, which this fix has no bearing on. No change to `runLargoQuery`/`runLargoQueryStream`'s in-turn Layer-4 verification (`verifyClaims(routed.answer, collectContextNumbers(routed.context))`, called with the unwrapped `routed.context` exactly as before) — only the persisted-for-later-audit copy changed shape.

**Fix rationale — why this design, what was deliberately left unchanged:** wrapping in a 1-element array (rather than, say, changing `tool_results`' column semantics to accept a bare object) keeps `RecentLargoAnswer.tool_results: unknown[]` and every consumer's array-shaped assumption (`collectContextNumbers`'s array branch, `auditLargoAnswerGrounding`'s signature) exactly as they already are — a caller-side fix, not a schema or read-side change. Left `tools_used: ["blackout_intelligence"]` untouched (still a sentinel, not a real tool name) — that's a `bie_interactions`-cohort concern (task #112-style calibration), orthogonal to this `largo_messages.tool_results` grounding-audit fix, and out of this task's scope per the task's own explicit instruction not to touch `bie/calibration.ts`.

**Verification:** `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Node 22.22.2 full suite (`node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`): **1518/1518 passing, 0 failures** (67 suites, 9 new: 4 in `largo-terminal.test.ts` extending the existing `appended` call-capture on the mocked `appendLargoMessage`, asserting the router branch now persists a non-empty `toolResults` array equal to the mocked composer's `context`, and that the pre-existing Claude-tool-loop `capturedResults` persistence is untouched; 5 new in `largo-store.test.ts`, 2 of which drive `appendLargoMessage()` directly through a mocked `dbClient` transaction and assert the INSERT's `tool_results` bind param is non-null JSON for a router-shaped call and still NULL when `toolResults` is omitted — closing the loop with the pre-existing read-side tests). Node 20.20.2 (identical invocation): **1518/1518 passing, 0 failures** — identical counts, no Node-20-vs-22 divergence. `npm run build` clean. `npm run lint:brand` / `npm run lint:vendor` both clean.

---

## Night Hawk admin dashboard gets a funnel/rejection-rate panel — task #145 (branch `fix/nighthawk-funnel-stats`)

**Status:** SHIPPED (draft PR). "Surface already-persisted data" task, not a new pipeline — every number this panel shows was already durably written before this PR, just never queried or rendered anywhere.

**Root cause / motivation (gap, not a bug):** Night Hawk's synthesis funnel (`claude-edition.ts`'s `generateEditionPlays`) makes a real publish-or-reject decision on every scored candidate, and (task #141/#142) every rejection at all 4 later-funnel stages — geometry, premium-cap, illiquid-strike, ungrounded, sector-concentration — is durably logged to `alert_audit_log` (`alert_type = 'nighthawk_rejected'`, deduped via `idx_alert_audit_log_nighthawk_rejected_dedup`), with a full confluence-factor breakdown folded into `input_snapshot.confluence` since task #142. None of that ever reached an operator's eyes: `AdminNightHawkDashboard.tsx` only ever fetched `/api/admin/nighthawk/analytics`, which calls `getNighthawkMetrics()` (`src/lib/nighthawk/analytics.ts`), which in turn only calls `fetchNighthawkOutcomeAnalytics()` — a query scoped entirely to `nighthawk_play_outcomes` (the PUBLISHED side). There was no code path anywhere that read `alert_audit_log` for `nighthawk_rejected` rows, so an operator had no way to see "how many candidates got rejected tonight, and why" without a manual SQL query — the exact "rejection rate over time" and "which gate is rejecting the most" questions this table was built to answer (per its own `docs/bie/AUDIT-TRAIL-SCHEMA.md` design intent) were invisible in the UI.

**What was built, following the existing `nighthawk_play_outcomes` fetch as the direct precedent (add a slice, don't rewrite the core):**
- `fetchNighthawkFunnelStats(windowDays)` (`src/lib/db.ts:5069`, right after `fetchNighthawkOutcomeAnalytics`) — two parallel read-only queries: `COUNT(*)` on `nighthawk_play_outcomes` in the window (published side — UNIQUE(edition_for, ticker), so this is the correct "candidate reached publication" count, deliberately NOT `alert_audit_log`'s own `alert_type = 'nighthawk'` count, which only logs a ticker's FIRST-ever publish via `recordNighthawkAuditTrail`'s `freshlyPublished` filter and would undercount a play carried over across editions); and `GROUP BY trigger_reason` on `alert_audit_log WHERE alert_type = 'nighthawk_rejected'` in the same window (rejected side — `trigger_reason` is a plain TEXT column, so grouping needs zero `decision_trace` JSON parsing). Same window-clamping backstop (`Number.isFinite`/`Math.trunc`) as its sibling function.
- `REJECTION_TRIGGER_REASON` (`src/lib/nighthawk/play-outcomes.ts:184`) — the existing internal map from rejection stage to its human-readable `trigger_reason` sentence (already the single source of truth for what gets written to the column above) — changed from module-private to `export`ed so the analytics layer can invert it instead of hardcoding a second copy of the 5 reason strings.
- `buildNighthawkFunnel(windowDays, publishedCount, rejectedByReason)` (`src/lib/nighthawk/analytics.ts`) — pure function, no DB access: inverts `REJECTION_TRIGGER_REASON` to label each rejected-reason row with a short stage slug (`geometry` -> `"Geometry"`, `premium_cap` -> `"Premium cap"`, etc., falling back to an `"other"` bucket for a future reason string this map doesn't recognize yet, rather than throwing), sums to `rejected_count`, and computes `candidates_count = published_count + rejected_count` and `rejection_rate = rejected_count / candidates_count` (0 when there were zero candidates, no divide-by-zero). Split from the DB read specifically so it's directly unit-testable, matching this file's existing convention (`entryMid`/`realizedReturnPct`/`avgLoserReturn` are all tested the same way in `analytics.test.ts`, no DB mocking needed).
- `NighthawkMetrics.funnel: NighthawkFunnelStats` — additive field on the existing analytics response; `getNighthawkMetrics()` now fetches `fetchNighthawkOutcomeAnalytics` and `fetchNighthawkFunnelStats` in parallel (`Promise.all`) so the new query adds no serial latency, and populates `funnel` on every return path including the empty-state (`emptyMetrics`).
- `AdminNightHawkDashboard.tsx` — new "Funnel · Rejection rate" `DeckPanel` (between the outcome-quality mega-stat row and "Conviction validation," reusing the exact `MegaStat`/`HorzBar`/`EmptyDeck` components and `admin-mega-grid`/`admin-nh-buckets` CSS classes the rest of the file already uses — no new styles): 4 `MegaStat` tiles (candidates considered, published, rejected, rejection rate) plus one `HorzBar` per rejection stage, sorted by count.

**Blast radius:** `src/lib/db.ts` (+1 function, `fetchNighthawkOutcomeAnalytics` and every other export byte-for-byte untouched), `src/lib/nighthawk/play-outcomes.ts` (1-line visibility change on an existing const — no behavior change, confirmed by the full existing `play-outcomes.test.ts`/`play-outcomes-stage-rejected.test.ts` suites passing unmodified), `src/lib/nighthawk/analytics.ts` (+1 type export, +1 pure function, +1 field on `NighthawkMetrics`, +1 parallel fetch in `getNighthawkMetrics`), `src/components/admin/AdminNightHawkDashboard.tsx` (+1 `DeckPanel`, no existing JSX touched). **Explicitly did NOT touch** `/api/market/nighthawk/record/route.ts` (the member-facing track-record route) — it already hand-picks which `NighthawkMetrics` fields to expose (`window_days`/`total_resolved`/`pending_count`/`win_rate_pct`/etc., not a spread), so the new `funnel` field is admin-only by construction, never reaches the member-facing payload.

**Fix rationale — what was deliberately left unchanged:** did not add a "total scored candidates" number to the funnel (i.e. candidates BEFORE the publish/reject decision) — that pool isn't durably logged anywhere today (only the two decision outcomes, publish and reject, are), so inventing a "candidates considered" number that included un-audited scoring-stage drops would be reporting a number the system doesn't actually have evidence for. `candidates_count` is explicitly documented as `published_count + rejected_count` (every candidate that reached a publish/reject decision), not the full scored pool. Kept the stage breakdown keyed off `trigger_reason` (SQL `GROUP BY`, already a plain column) rather than parsing each row's `decision_trace` JSON — cheaper, simpler, and exactly as precise since `trigger_reason` and `decision_trace`'s stage are set from the same discriminated union at write time (`buildNighthawkStageRejectedAuditRow`).

**Evidence / verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty. 6 new pure-function tests in `analytics.test.ts` (`buildNighthawkFunnel`): zero-candidates no-divide-by-zero, all-published zero-rejection-rate, mixed publish/reject rate math (8 published + 2 rejected = 20% rejection rate), every one of the 5 real `REJECTION_TRIGGER_REASON` stages resolves to a real non-"other" humanized label (regression guard against a silent map mismatch), sort-by-count-descending, and an unrecognized `trigger_reason` falls back to an `"other"` bucket instead of throwing. Full suite (`node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`) on Node 22.22.2: **1522/1522 passing, 0 failures** (66 suites, 6 net-new). Same suite on Node 20.20.2: **1522/1522 passing, 0 failures** — identical pass count on both runtimes. `npm run build` clean (`✓ Compiled successfully`). `npm run lint:brand` / `npm run lint:vendor` both clean.

---

## BIE self-eval: market_context (the 4th router intent) gets its own tool-calling calibration cohort — task #161 (branch `fix/bie-market-context-calibration-cohort`)

**Status:** SHIPPED (draft PR). Direct sibling of task #112 (SPX Slayer's own tool-calling cohort, entry further below) and task #149 (0DTE Command's, entry below) — same gap, same UNION-membership architecture, applied to `market_context`, the FOURTH and last of BIE's deterministic router intents left without a cohort (`zerodte_plays`/`ticker_play_state`/`spx_structure` already had theirs via tasks #112/#149; `spx_structure` is folded into the SPX cohort). Enhancement, not a bug fix: closes a BIE-observability gap, never touches a live gate/score/action.

**Root cause / motivation (gap, not a bug):** before this PR, `bie/calibration.ts` had five passes — 0DTE Command's `zerodte_setup_log`, SPX Slayer's own closed-play P&L (`spx_play_outcomes`), and tasks #112/#133/#137/#144/#149's tool-calling answer-quality cohorts for SPX Slayer/HELIX/Thermal/Night Hawk/0DTE Command — but nothing measured whether Largo's ANSWERS were any good on turns that touched `market_context`'s own composed state (SPX/VIX/SPY/QQQ/IWM/SOXX snapshots, UW market tide, market status, upcoming sessions, the user's own SPX desk summary — see `run-tool.ts`'s `"get_market_context"` case and `bie/composers.ts`'s `composeMarketContext`). `market_context` is notable among the five prior cohorts because it — like `zerodte_plays`/`spx_structure` — IS a real deterministic router intent (`src/lib/bie/router.ts`'s `classifyBieIntent`: exactly 4 intents exist — `zerodte_plays`, `ticker_play_state`, `spx_structure`, `market_context` — and `market_context` was the one intent BIE could route to for free but never separately measured). Grounding-verifier claim correctness (`claims_verified`/`claims_total`) says nothing about this on its own: every numeric claim in an answer can be individually grounded/true while the answer still reads a stale snapshot or dispatches the wrong tool.

**Cohort-membership decision (verified against `run-tool.ts`'s and `composers.ts`'s actual code, not guessed from naming):**
- `MARKET_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` (`src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts`, right after `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`) = `["get_market_context"]` — a single-tool list, deliberately narrower than the task brief's "likely `["get_market_context", "get_market_regime"]`" guess. Verified by reading both tools' `run-tool.ts` case bodies directly: `get_market_context`'s case is EXACTLY what `composeMarketContext` (`bie/composers.ts`) reads via `runLargoTool("get_market_context", {})` to answer the `market_context` router intent — the same one-tool relationship `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`'s `get_spx_structure` has to `composeSpxStructure`. `get_market_regime`'s case instead calls `fetchPlatformIntelSnapshot()` (`src/lib/nighthawk/platform-intel-snapshot.ts`) — a COMPLETELY DIFFERENT read that `composeMarketContext` never touches. `get_market_regime` is deliberately excluded for the exact reason `HELIX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`'s own doc comment already excludes it from ITS list: it's a `BIE_TOOL_NAMES` member precisely because it's cross-product/callable for ANY question, and `bie_interactions.tools_used` records only tool NAMES, never call scope — including it here would silently admit turns that never touched `market_context`'s own composed state into this cohort. `tool-defs.test.ts` pins this: real-tool check, subset-of-`TOOL_GROUPS.vol_analysis` check (where `get_market_context` itself lives — NOT `TOOL_GROUPS.platform`, unlike the SPX/0DTE/Night-Hawk cohorts), an exact-single-element-set assertion, an explicit `get_market_regime`-exclusion test with the full rationale inline, and no-duplicates.
- **Cohort test is a UNION, not a pure `tools_used` check — same reasoning as task #112's SPX cohort and task #149's 0DTE cohort, NOT the pure-`tools_used` pattern used for HELIX/Thermal/Night Hawk (which have no router intent).** `market_context`'s router answer (`composeMarketContext`) reads the exact same `get_market_context` state a Claude-tool-calling turn would read, but `logBie()` always records that router path's `tools_used` as the `["blackout_intelligence"]` sentinel, never the real tool name (same mechanism task #112 documented for SPX Slayer's `spx_structure` intent). Fix: cohort membership = `tools_used ∩ MARKET_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES ≠ ∅` **OR** `intent_bucket === "market_context"` (`isMarketContextToolCallingRow`, `calibration.ts`; SQL-layer equivalent `tools_used ?| $2::text[] OR intent_bucket = 'market_context'` in `fetchMarketContextToolCallingBieInteractions`, `db.ts`). Without this OR, `router_match_rate_pct` would read a permanent, meaningless 0% purely as an artifact of how `tools_used` is populated on the router path, not because router coverage is actually bad.

**What was built, following task #112's/#149's exact "add a slice, don't rewrite the core" pattern (types → cohort predicate → pure compute → format → DB-backed orchestrator → wiring), placed immediately after the task #149 section and clearly marked "Task #161" in `src/lib/bie/calibration.ts`:**
- `MARKET_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` (`src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts`, right after `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`).
- `fetchMarketContextToolCallingBieInteractions(sinceDate, marketEngineToolNames, limit = 3000)` (`src/lib/db.ts`, right after `fetchZeroDteToolCallingBieInteractions`) — same SQL-layer cohort filtering rationale as every prior fetcher (`bie_interactions` is one row per Largo QUESTION, much higher volume than the admission-gated tables the other fetchers read), UNION SQL identical in shape to the SPX/0DTE fetchers.
- `MarketContextToolCallInputRow`, `MarketContextToolCallCalibrationReport`, `isMarketContextToolCallingRow()`, `computeMarketContextToolCallCalibration()`, `formatMarketContextToolCallCalibration()`, `computeMarketContextToolCallCalibrationFromDb()` (`src/lib/bie/calibration.ts`) — same bucket math, same `MIN_EVIDENCE = 10` evidence-gating, same fail-open (`try {} catch { return null }`) contract as every sibling cohort.
- `CalibrationReport.market_context_tool_calls: MarketContextToolCallCalibrationReport | null` — additive field; `computeCalibration()` sets it `null` alongside the other five tool-calling slices (same "attached later by the async orchestrator" comment); `runBieCalibration()` attaches it as a sixth, independent pass; `formatCalibration()` appends a sixth `---`-separated section when present.
- **Wiring, zero new surface needed:** `GET /api/admin/bie-report` and `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` already render `formatCalibration`'s raw text, so the new section appears automatically.

**Evidence (test run, this PR):**
- 9 new pure-math tests in `bie.test.ts` (mirroring task #112's/#149's SPX/0DTE-tool-call test blocks structurally): cohort inclusion (`tools_used` intersection with `MARKET_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`) / exclusion (generic-only tools); a router-matched `market_context` row joining the cohort despite the `["blackout_intelligence"]` sentinel (and a `spx_structure` router match correctly NOT joining — the cross-product negative case); aggregate math over a mixed cohort (weighted grounding ratio 12/15=80%, avg latency rounding to 3347ms); `claims_total=0` rows counted in `n` but excluded from the grounding ratio; evidence-gating (`n<10` → zero recommendations); recommendations firing once `n≥10` (both low-grounding and low-router-match-rate); empty-cohort → `null` rates; a 5-way combined-report text assertion and the "no sixth section when unattached" regression.
- 5 new tests in `tool-defs.test.ts`: `MARKET_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` is a real `LARGO_TOOL_DEFS` tool, is a subset of `TOOL_GROUPS.vol_analysis`, is exactly `["get_market_context"]`, excludes `get_market_regime` (with the full rationale asserted inline), and has no duplicates.
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: clean.
- Node 22.22.2 full suite (`/opt/node22/bin/node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`): **1530/1530 passing, 0 failures** (66 suites).
- Node 20.20.2 (`/opt/node20/bin/node`, identical invocation): **1530/1530 passing, 0 failures** — identical counts on both runtimes, no Node-20-vs-22 divergence for this change.
- `npm run build`: clean (exit 0, full route manifest generated).
- `npm run lint:brand` / `npm run lint:vendor`: both clean.
- `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts`: empty — this PR never touches the live SPX play-engine, and never touches `market_context`'s own read path (`run-tool.ts`'s `"get_market_context"` case, `composers.ts`'s `composeMarketContext`) either — both are read-only consumers here, untouched.

**Blast radius:** `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts` (+1 exported const + its doc comment, immediately after `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` — no existing export touched), `src/lib/db.ts` (+1 function, `fetchMarketContextToolCallingBieInteractions` — every existing exported function byte-for-byte untouched), `src/lib/bie/calibration.ts` (+1 type pair, +1 membership predicate, +1 pure compute function, +1 formatter, +1 DB orchestrator, +1 field on `CalibrationReport`, +1 null-attach in `computeCalibration`, +1 wiring line in `runBieCalibration`, +1 append in `formatCalibration` — every existing pass's own code path untouched), `src/lib/bie/bie.test.ts` (+9 new cases), `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.test.ts` (+5 new cases). No router, composer, or live-engine file touched — `router.ts`'s `classifyBieIntent`/`MARKET_CONTEXT_RE` and `composers.ts`'s `composeMarketContext` are read-only consumers referenced only in comments.

**Fix rationale — why this design, what was deliberately left unchanged:** followed task #112's/#149's precedent exactly rather than inventing a new shape, per the task's explicit instruction — a sixth pure-function-plus-format-plus-orchestrator slice, independently testable and independently failure-isolated from the five existing passes. Chose the UNION-membership pattern (not the pure-`tools_used` pattern used for HELIX/Thermal/Night Hawk) specifically because `market_context` genuinely has a deterministic router path to account for, unlike those three products — using the wrong pattern here would have made `router_match_rate_pct` read a permanent, dishonest 0% for a router path that actually exists. Declined the task brief's suggested `get_market_regime` inclusion after verifying it reads an unrelated backend function — including a tool the composer never calls would have silently widened the cohort to unrelated cross-product turns, the same mistake `HELIX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`'s own doc comment already warns against for the identical tool. Left `MIN_EVIDENCE`, the grounding-ratio weighting convention, and the recommendation-threshold philosophy (< 70% grounding, < 30% router-match) identical to every existing pass rather than inventing new numbers.

## BIE self-eval: Night's-Watch-tool-calling Largo turns get their own calibration cohort — task #163 (branch `fix/bie-nights-watch-calibration-cohort`)

**Status:** SHIPPED (draft PR). Direct analogue of task #112 (SPX-tool-calling cohort, entry further below) and, architecturally, the closest sibling of task #144 (Night Hawk, entry below) — same tools_used-only asymmetry, applied to Night's Watch (the signed-in user's own per-position options book, `/account`'s positions panel) instead of SPX Slayer or Night Hawk. Same `calibration.ts`/`db.ts`/`tool-defs.ts` file group the other five sibling PRs touch — merge-order conflicts there are expected and a human's call to resolve, not this branch's.

**Root cause / motivation (gap, not a bug):** Night's Watch already has its own data-correctness verifier — `src/lib/correctness/nights-watch-verifier.ts` (priority correctness surface #3, registered in `run-correctness.ts`) — that shadow-recomputes P&L/breakeven/DTE from scratch and chain-cross-confirms every held contract against the shared chain cache. But that verifier only proves the served **valuation FORMULA** is correct; before this PR, `bie/calibration.ts` had no way to measure whether Largo's **ANSWERS** about that same engine state — specifically the deterministic Hold/Trim/Sell verdict `get_my_positions` hands back, a proprietary derived judgment analogous to SPX Slayer's grade/action — were actually GOOD answers. Grounding-verifier claim-by-claim numeric correctness says nothing about this on its own: every dollar figure in an answer can be individually grounded/true while the answer still misreads which leg is which, serves a stale verdict, or dispatches the tool with the wrong `status` filter. This is the exact same blind spot task #112 closed for SPX Slayer, never yet extended to this product.

**Cohort-membership decision (verified against source, not guessed from naming):**
- `NIGHTS_WATCH_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` (`src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts`, right after `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`) = `["get_my_positions"]`. Confirmed via `run-tool.ts`'s `"get_my_positions"` case body: it calls `getEnrichedPositionsForUser(userId, statusArg)` (`src/lib/nights-watch/enrichment.ts`), which reads the user's saved positions and re-derives each one's live valuation plus the deterministic verdict (`src/lib/nights-watch/verdict.ts`) — exactly the state `nights-watch-verifier.ts` already audits the formula for. `TOOL_GROUPS.my_book` contains exactly this one tool (confirmed by reading `tool-defs.ts`'s `TOOL_GROUPS` object directly), so — like `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` — this is not a *narrowing* of a larger bundle; it is simply that one tool, with no generic ticker-agnostic tool bundled in alongside it to exclude.
- **Checked for a companion tool and found none, per the task's explicit ask to verify rather than assume:** `src/lib/nights-watch/position-detail.ts`'s `buildPositionDetail()` looked like a plausible `get_position_detail` companion (it recomputes the same verdict with richer per-position context for the account panel's click→detail modal) — but `grep -rn "position_detail\|positionDetail" src/lib/largo/` turns up nothing in `tool-defs.ts` or `run-tool.ts`. It is wired directly to a plain Next.js API route (`src/app/api/account/positions/[id]/detail/route.ts`, confirmed via `grep -rn buildPositionDetail src/`), never to a Largo tool, so there is no companion tool name to add. Locked in with a dedicated `tool-defs.test.ts` assertion (`get_position_detail` is not a registered `LARGO_TOOL_DEFS` entry) so a future reader doesn't "fix" this by inventing one.
- **Cohort test is tools_used-ONLY, deliberately NOT a UNION** — the same asymmetry task #144 documented for Night Hawk, verified independently here: `classifyBieIntent` (`src/lib/bie/router.ts`) recognizes exactly 4 deterministic intents (`zerodte_plays`, `ticker_play_state`, `spx_structure`, `market_context`) and none of them route a Night's-Watch/"my positions" question — there is no deterministic BIE router answer path for it at all. `MY_POSITIONS_RE` (`largo/intent-keywords.ts`) looks like it could play the same role `SPX_STRUCTURE_RE` plays for the SPX cohort, but it does a different job entirely: it only decides which TOOL BUNDLE Largo has on hand for a question (`getToolsForIntent`), and is never consulted by `classifyBieIntent`'s answer path. Consequence: `router_matched_n`/`router_match_rate_pct` on this cohort read a legitimate, permanent `0` today — not a bug, not something to paper over with a fabricated `OR intent_bucket = '...'` clause (both `fetchNightsWatchToolCallingBieInteractions`'s and `isNightsWatchToolCallingRow`'s doc comments say so explicitly). Also **omitted the low-router-match-rate recommendation** the SPX pass has, for the same reason task #144 omitted it: a permanent 0% would fire on every report forever and teach a reader to tune it out.

**What was built, following `calibration.ts`'s own established "add a slice, don't rewrite the core" pattern (`computeSpxToolCallCalibration` from task #112 was the direct precedent, `computeNighthawkToolCallCalibration` from task #144 the closer architectural sibling):**
- `computeNightsWatchToolCallCalibration(rows, window)` — pure, exported, directly unit-tested (no DB mocking needed): reports `n`, `claude_fallback_n`/`router_matched_n`/`router_match_rate_pct` (the latter two honestly 0 today), `grounding_pass_rate_pct` (aggregate `sum(claims_verified)/sum(claims_total)` over cohort rows that carried claims, weighted by claim count), `avg_latency_ms`, and evidence-gated `recommendations` (same `MIN_EVIDENCE = 10` bar every other pass uses).
- `isNightsWatchToolCallingRow()` — the tools_used-only membership predicate.
- `formatNightsWatchToolCallCalibration(r)` — human-readable summary, same convention as `formatNighthawkToolCallCalibration`.
- `CalibrationReport.nights_watch_tool_calls: NightsWatchToolCallCalibrationReport | null` — additive field, same attach-after-compute pattern as the other five slices. `formatCalibration` appends an extra `---`-separated section when present; a report without it renders identically to before (regression-tested).
- `computeNightsWatchToolCallCalibrationFromDb` (non-exported async orchestrator, same fail-open `try {} catch { return null }` contract as every sibling `*FromDb` helper) + `fetchNightsWatchToolCallingBieInteractions` (new, `src/lib/db.ts`, right after `fetchZeroDteToolCallingBieInteractions` — SQL-layer `tools_used ?| $2::text[]` filter only, no `OR intent_bucket = ...` clause, per the asymmetry above) — wired into `runBieCalibration(days = 14)` as a sixth pass alongside the existing five.
- **Wiring, zero new surface needed:** `GET /api/admin/bie-report` and `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` already render `formatCalibration`'s raw text, so the new section appears automatically once `runBieCalibration` populates `nights_watch_tool_calls`.

**Evidence (test run, this PR):**
- 10 new pure-math tests in `bie.test.ts` mirroring task #144's Night Hawk test block structurally: cohort inclusion (`tools_used` intersection with `NIGHTS_WATCH_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`) / exclusion (generic-only tools); an explicit "intent_bucket alone, without a matching tool, never joins the cohort" test (locks in the tools_used-only asymmetry); aggregate math over a mixed cohort (`sum(verified)/sum(total)` = 12/15 = 80%, weighted; avg latency = (4000+6000+40)/3 → 3347ms); `claims_total=0` rows counted in `n` but excluded from the grounding ratio; evidence-gating at n=5 (no recommendations), n=10 (gate clears, low-grounding recommendation fires, no router-coverage recommendation), and n=11; empty-cohort → `null` rates; a combined-report test asserting all six sections render together; and the "no extra section when unattached" regression.
- 5 new tests in `tool-defs.test.ts`: every `NIGHTS_WATCH_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` entry is a real `LARGO_TOOL_DEFS` tool, is a subset of `TOOL_GROUPS.my_book`, is exactly the `{get_my_positions}` singleton, `get_position_detail` is confirmed NOT a registered tool (the companion-tool check), and no duplicates.
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: clean.
- Node 22.22.2 (`/opt/node22/bin/node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`): **1531/1531 passing, 0 failures** (66 suites).
- Node 20.20.2 (`/opt/node20/bin/node`, identical invocation): **1531/1531 passing, 0 failures** — identical count on both runtimes, no Node-version-specific gap on this branch.
- `npm run build`: clean (`✓ Compiled successfully`). `npm run lint:brand` / `npm run lint:vendor`: both clean.
- `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts`: empty — confirmed before opening this PR; this task never touches the live SPX play-engine, and never touches Night's Watch's own valuation/verdict computation either (`src/lib/nights-watch/enrichment.ts`, `src/lib/nights-watch/verdict.ts`, `nights-watch-verifier.ts` are all read-only consumers here, untouched).

**Fix rationale — why this design, what was deliberately left unchanged:** reused task #112's architecture exactly (types → predicate → pure compute → format → DB orchestrator → wiring) rather than inventing a new shape, per the task's explicit instruction to mirror the existing pattern — and specifically reused task #144's tools_used-only asymmetry (rather than task #149's UNION) because Night's Watch, like Night Hawk, genuinely has no deterministic router intent to work around. Declined to add a `classifyBieIntent` branch for Night's Watch to make `router_matched_n` look better — that's a materially bigger, riskier scope change (new regex, new routing behavior over real per-user financial data) than this cohort-visibility task calls for, and a fabricated OR-clause with no real router path behind it would have been actively dishonest about router coverage. Left `MIN_EVIDENCE`, the grounding-ratio weighting convention, and the recommendation-threshold philosophy identical to the other five passes rather than inventing new numbers. `spx-signals.ts`, `nights-watch-verifier.ts`, and every live valuation/verdict code path are untouched; this is a sixth, independent, read-only analytics pass over `bie_interactions`.

**Blast radius:** `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts` (+1 export, `NIGHTS_WATCH_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`, purely additive), `src/lib/db.ts` (+1 function, `fetchNightsWatchToolCallingBieInteractions`, nothing existing touched), `src/lib/bie/calibration.ts` (+1 additive `CalibrationReport` field, +1 type pair, +1 predicate, +1 compute fn, +1 format fn, +1 DB orchestrator, +2 lines in `runBieCalibration`/`computeCalibration` — every existing field/function byte-for-byte unchanged), `src/lib/bie/bie.test.ts` (+10 tests), `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.test.ts` (+5 tests). No other file touched.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite 1531/1531 passing on both Node 22.22.2 and Node 20.20.2 (15 new tests: 10 in `bie.test.ts`, 5 in `tool-defs.test.ts`). `npm run build` clean. `lint:brand`/`lint:vendor` clean. `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty.
