## BIE self-eval: Thermal-tool-calling Largo turns get their own calibration cohort — task #137 (branch `fix/thermal-bie-calibration-cohort`)

**Status:** SHIPPED. Direct sibling of task #112 (SPX Slayer's own tool-calling cohort, entry below) — same gap, same architecture, applied to BlackOut Thermal (the dealer-GEX/positioning product behind `/heatmap`) instead of SPX Slayer. Enhancement, not a bug fix: closes a BIE-observability gap, never touches a live gate/score/action. `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (confirmed before opening this PR).

**Root cause / motivation (gap, not a bug):** before this PR, `bie/calibration.ts` had four passes — 0DTE Command's `zerodte_setup_log`, SPX Slayer's own closed-play P&L (`spx_play_outcomes`), and task #112's SPX-tool-calling answer-quality cohort — but nothing measured whether Largo's ANSWERS were any good on turns that touched **Thermal's** own computed state (dealer positioning, gamma flip, call/put walls, max pain, GEX king strike, net GEX/VEX/DEX/CHARM, and — since task #136 shipped `gex_regime_events` — the regime/flip/wall-crossing transition history too). Grounding-verifier claim correctness (`claims_verified`/`claims_total`) says nothing about this on its own: every numeric claim in an answer can be individually grounded/true while the answer still reads a stale matrix, misreads which wall is which, or dispatches the wrong Thermal tool entirely.

**Cohort-membership decision (documented per the task's explicit ask for judgment + rationale, verified against `run-tool.ts`'s actual case bodies, not guessed from naming):**
- `THERMAL_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` (`src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts`) = `["get_positioning", "get_gex_regime_events"]` — a narrower list than `TOOL_GROUPS.stock_analysis` (both names are members of it), kept to the tools whose `run-tool.ts` implementation actually reads Thermal's own shared cache. `get_positioning` → `fetchPositioningSummary` (`src/lib/nighthawk/positioning.ts`) → primary path calls `getGexPositioning(sym)` (`src/lib/providers/gex-positioning.ts`), a strict cache-reader over `fetchGexHeatmap`'s shared `gex-heatmap:{ticker}` matrix — that file's own header comment calls it "the ONE source every other tool/service/AI surface consumes for the Heat Maps dealer-positioning data." `get_gex_regime_events` → `gexRegimeEventsForLargo` (`src/lib/providers/gex-regime-events.ts`) reads `gex_regime_events`, task #136's durable log persisted directly off `computeGexEvents()`'s diff of that SAME `fetchGexHeatmap` matrix.
  - **The naming trap, called out explicitly so a future reader doesn't "fix" it:** `get_gex` was deliberately **excluded**, despite Thermal literally being "the GEX product." Read its `run-tool.ts` case body end to end: for SPX/I:SPX at today's expiry it reads `getLargoSpxLiveDesk(userId)` — SPX SLAYER's own live desk, `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`' territory, not Thermal's. For every other case it calls `fetchPolygonOdteGexRows` → `fetchPolygonOdteDeskBundle`, a THIRD, separate, spot-keyed cache (no ticker parameter at all — it's the 0DTE desk bundle) — or, failing that, raw ad hoc Unusual Whales spot-exposure/gex-level calls. None of `get_gex`'s three branches ever call `fetchGexHeatmap`/`getGexPositioning`. This is the same "too generic, verified via the case body" reasoning task #112's own doc comment used to exclude `get_gex` from the SPX list — it just lands on the identical exclusion here for a different, Thermal-specific reason (it doesn't read Thermal's state either).
  - Also excluded, and verified via their case bodies: `get_options_chain`/`get_oi_per_strike`/`get_max_pain`/`get_greeks`/`get_atm_chains`/`get_options_volume` (all `TOOL_GROUPS.stock_analysis`) — each independently fetches+computes over a raw `polygonChainBundle()` chain for whatever ticker/expiry was asked, never reading the shared heatmap cache. Also excluded `get_ecosystem_context` (`BIE_TOOL_NAMES`): its payload does embed a `gex_positioning` field (the same `getGexPositioning()` object `get_positioning` returns), but it's cross-product/any-ticker and `bie_interactions.tools_used` only records tool NAMES, never call inputs — same reasoning task #112 used to exclude it from the SPX list.
  - `tool-defs.test.ts` pins all of this: real-tool / subset-of-`stock_analysis` / excludes-`get_gex` (with its own dedicated test explaining why) / excludes-the-6-generic-chain-tools / excludes-`get_ecosystem_context` / no-dupes.
- **Cohort test is a PLAIN `tools_used` check — no `intent_bucket` OR-clause, the deliberate asymmetry vs. task #112.** `classifyBieIntent` (`src/lib/bie/router.ts`) recognizes exactly 4 intents — `zerodte_plays`, `ticker_play_state`, `spx_structure`, `market_context` — and **none of them is Thermal/GEX-flavored**. Task #112's SPX cohort needs its `OR intent_bucket = 'spx_structure'` clause because the router's `spx_structure` composer internally calls the exact same SPX-engine read a Claude tool-calling turn would make, but `logBie()` always logs that path's `tools_used` as the `["blackout_intelligence"]` sentinel — without the OR, the SPX cohort would silently exclude every router-matched row. Thermal has no equivalent router path to work around: every Thermal-engine-tool turn necessarily went through Claude's tool-calling loop and logged the real tool name, so a plain `tools_used` check already sees the complete cohort. Per this task's explicit instruction, adding a deterministic Thermal/GEX router intent (new classification regexes, a new composer, new follow-up chips) was intentionally NOT done here — that's a materially bigger scope change than a calibration-cohort task calls for. The direct, honest consequence: `computeThermalToolCallCalibration`'s `router_matched_n` legitimately reads 0 today. `isThermalToolCallingRow`'s own doc comment says this explicitly, so a future reader doesn't "fix" the 0% by fabricating an `intent_bucket` match.

**What was built (`src/lib/bie/calibration.ts`, following the file's own established "add a slice, don't rewrite the core" pattern — task #112's `computeSpxToolCallCalibration` was the direct precedent):**
- `computeThermalToolCallCalibration(rows, window)` — pure, exported, directly unit-tested (no DB mocking needed, same as every sibling pass): reports `n`, `claude_fallback_n`/`router_matched_n`/`router_match_rate_pct` (the latter two honestly 0 today per the asymmetry above), `grounding_pass_rate_pct` (aggregate `sum(claims_verified)/sum(claims_total)` over cohort rows that carried claims, weighted by claim count), `avg_latency_ms`, and evidence-gated `recommendations` (same `MIN_EVIDENCE = 10` bar every other pass uses).
- `formatThermalToolCallCalibration(r)` — human-readable summary, same convention as `formatSpxToolCallCalibration`.
- `CalibrationReport.thermal_tool_calls: ThermalToolCallCalibrationReport | null` — additive field, same attach-after-compute pattern as `spx_tool_calls`. `formatCalibration` now appends a fourth `---`-separated section when present; a report without it renders identically to before (regression-tested).
- `computeThermalToolCallCalibrationFromDb` (non-exported async orchestrator, same fail-open `try {} catch { return null }` contract as `computeSpxToolCallCalibrationFromDb`) + `fetchThermalToolCallingBieInteractions` (new, `src/lib/db.ts`) — wired into `runBieCalibration(days = 14)` alongside the existing three passes, same rolling window. Cohort filtering runs at the SQL layer (`tools_used ?| $2::text[]`, Postgres `jsonb ?|` array-overlap operator) for the same reason task #112's fetcher does: `bie_interactions` is one row per Largo QUESTION — much higher volume than the admission-gated tables the other two fetchers read.
- **Wiring, zero new surface needed:** `GET /api/admin/bie-report` already returns `{ data: calibration, text: formatCalibration(calibration) }`, and `AdminBieDashboard.tsx`'s "Gate calibration · 14 sessions" panel already renders `data?.calibration?.text` as raw `<pre>` text — the new section appears automatically once `runBieCalibration` populates `thermal_tool_calls`; no route or dashboard component change needed.

**Blast radius:** `src/lib/db.ts` (+1 function, `fetchThermalToolCallingBieInteractions` — `fetchSpxToolCallingBieInteractions` and every other exported function byte-for-byte untouched), `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts` (+1 exported const + its doc comment, immediately after `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` — no existing export touched), `src/lib/bie/calibration.ts` (+1 type pair, +1 membership predicate, +1 pure compute function, +1 formatter, +1 DB orchestrator, +1 field on `CalibrationReport`, +1 null-attach in `computeCalibration`, +1 wiring line in `runBieCalibration`, +1 append in `formatCalibration` — every existing pass's own code path untouched), `src/lib/bie/bie.test.ts` (+11 new cases), `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.test.ts` (+6 new cases). **Concurrency note:** `calibration.ts`, `db.ts`, and `tool-defs.ts` are being edited concurrently by 3 sibling PRs for tasks #133/#144/#149 doing this exact same pattern for other products — merge conflicts on these three files are expected and will be resolved by a human, not by this PR.

**Fix rationale — why this design, what was deliberately left unchanged:** followed task #112's precedent exactly (a fourth pure-function-plus-format-plus-orchestrator slice, not a rewrite of any existing pass) so all four passes stay independently testable and independently failure-isolated — a bug in the new Thermal fetch can never take down the 0DTE, SPX-outcomes, or SPX-tool-calling sections. Kept `THERMAL_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` as an explicit literal list rather than deriving it from `TOOL_GROUPS.stock_analysis` (e.g. by filtering for a "positioning" tag), same reasoning `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` gives, so the cohort can't silently widen/narrow if `stock_analysis`'s bundle composition changes for unrelated routing reasons. Deliberately did NOT add a Thermal/GEX router intent to `classifyBieIntent` to make `router_matched_n` look better — that's explicitly out of scope per the task, and fabricating router coverage would corrupt the exact metric this cohort exists to report honestly.

**Evidence / verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty. Full suite (`node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`) on Node 22.22.2: **1475/1475 passing, 0 failures** (66 suites) — no pre-existing failures found on this branch's baseline. Same suite on Node 20.20.2 (`nvm use 20`, CI's exact glob-expansion invocation): **1475/1475 passing, 0 failures** — identical pass count on both runtimes, no Node-20-only regressions. `npm run build` clean (`✓ Compiled successfully`, full route manifest generated). `npm run lint:brand` clean (`no grey-family color classes in src/`). `npm run lint:vendor` clean (`no provider names in scanned user-facing UI`).

## BIE self-eval: Night-Hawk-tool-calling Largo turns get their own calibration cohort — task #144 (branch `fix/nighthawk-bie-calibration-cohort`)

**Status:** SHIPPED (draft PR). Direct analogue of task #112 (SPX-tool-calling cohort, entry further below), applied to Night Hawk — same architecture, reused deliberately rather than reinvented, per the task brief. Concurrent sibling PRs (tasks #133/#137/#149) apply the identical pattern to other products in `calibration.ts`/`db.ts`/`tool-defs.ts` at the same time — merge conflicts there are expected and left for a human to resolve, not this PR.

**Root cause / motivation (gap, not a bug):** before this PR, `bie/calibration.ts` had no way to measure "how good are Largo's ANSWERS specifically when Night Hawk's own engine-state tools were involved" — the same blind spot task #112 closed for SPX Slayer, just never extended to the platform's other flagship product. Night Hawk already has its own outcome tracking (`nighthawk_play_outcomes`, surfaced via `get_nighthawk_outcomes`/`get_spx_vs_nighthawk_comparison`) and BIE's grounding verifier already checks claim-by-claim numeric correctness — but neither one says anything about whether Largo picked the right Night Hawk tool, read the right edition, or served a stale-but-technically-accurate dossier. This PR gives that specific cohort continuous visibility, same evidence-gating philosophy (n≥10/bucket) as every other pass on this report.

**Cohort-membership decision (documented per this task's explicit ask for judgment + rationale, mirroring task #112's own documented decision):**
- `NIGHTHAWK_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` (`src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts`) is a **narrower** list than `TOOL_GROUPS.platform`'s Night-Hawk-flavored entries, kept to tools whose `run-tool.ts` case body verifiably reads Night Hawk's own persisted/computed state: `get_nighthawk_edition` (`marketPlatform.nighthawk.getLatestNightHawkEdition()`/`getNightHawkEditionForDate()` — the published edition object), `get_nighthawk_outcomes` (`fetchNighthawkOutcomeAnalytics()`+`fetchPendingNighthawkOutcomes()` — Night Hawk's own closed/pending outcome ledger), `get_nighthawk_dossier` (`fetchStagedDossiers()` falling back to `fetchNighthawkScoringHistory()` — Night Hawk's own per-ticker research/scoring state). All three verified by reading their `run-tool.ts` case statements directly, not guessed from naming.
- **Excluded `get_spx_vs_nighthawk_comparison`** even though `TOOL_GROUPS.platform` lists it alongside the other three: its `run-tool.ts` case body ALWAYS calls both `fetchPlayOutcomeStatsForWindow` (SPX Slayer) AND `fetchNighthawkOutcomeAnalytics` (Night Hawk) and returns a derived cross-product delta. A turn calling only this tool touched SPX Slayer's own engine state exactly as certainly as Night Hawk's — including it would have silently CONFLATED this cohort with SPX-engine-state turns, the same failure mode task #112's own comment warns against for `get_ecosystem_context`, just approached from the opposite direction (there, ambiguous scope; here, certain dual-scope).
- **Also excluded `get_platform_snapshot`**, for the same "can't attribute scope from `tools_used` alone" reasoning: it's a cross-service combo across up to 3 products gated by its own `include`/`full_edition` params, but `bie_interactions.tools_used` records only the tool NAME, never call inputs — a logged row can't reveal whether a given call ever touched the `nighthawk` slice at all (it may have been `include: ["spx","flows"]` only).
- **Cohort test is `tools_used`-ONLY, deliberately NOT a UNION** — the inverse of task #112's non-obvious part. `classifyBieIntent` (`bie/router.ts`) recognizes exactly 4 deterministic intents (`zerodte_plays`, `ticker_play_state`, `spx_structure`, `market_context`) and none of them route Night Hawk questions — there is no deterministic BIE router answer path for Night Hawk at all. `NIGHTHAWK_RE` (`largo/intent-keywords.ts`) looks like it could play the same role `SPX_STRUCTURE_RE` plays for the SPX cohort, but it does a different job entirely: it only decides which TOOL BUNDLE Largo has on hand for a question (`getToolsForIntent`), and is never consulted by `classifyBieIntent`'s answer path. Consequence: `router_matched_n`/`router_match_rate_pct` on this cohort read a legitimate, permanent `0` today — not a bug, not something to paper over with a fabricated `OR intent_bucket = '...'` clause (both `fetchNighthawkToolCallingBieInteractions`'s and `isNighthawkToolCallingRow`'s doc comments say so explicitly, so a future reader doesn't "fix" this by inventing a fake match). Also **omitted the low-router-match-rate recommendation** SPX's pass has: with `router_matched_n` structurally always 0, that recommendation would fire on every single report forever, teaching a human reader to tune it out — it would never carry new information. If a real Night Hawk router intent is ever added, both the SQL OR-clause and this recommendation should be added together at that point (documented inline).

**What was built, following `calibration.ts`'s own established "add a slice, don't rewrite the core" pattern (`computeSpxToolCallCalibration` from task #112 was the direct precedent):**
- `computeNighthawkToolCallCalibration(rows, window)` — pure, exported, directly unit-tested (no DB mocking needed): reports `n`, `claude_fallback_n`/`router_matched_n`/`router_match_rate_pct` (the latter two honestly 0 today), `grounding_pass_rate_pct` (aggregate `sum(claims_verified)/sum(claims_total)` over cohort rows that carried claims, weighted by claim count), `avg_latency_ms`, and evidence-gated `recommendations` (same `MIN_EVIDENCE = 10` bar).
- `formatNighthawkToolCallCalibration(r)` — human-readable summary, same convention as `formatSpxToolCallCalibration`.
- `CalibrationReport.nighthawk_tool_calls: NighthawkToolCallCalibrationReport | null` — additive field, same attach-after-compute pattern as `spx_tool_calls`. `formatCalibration` appends a fourth `---`-separated section when present; a report without it renders identically to before (regression-tested).
- `computeNighthawkToolCallCalibrationFromDb` (non-exported async orchestrator, same fail-open `try {} catch { return null }` contract as its SPX sibling) + `fetchNighthawkToolCallingBieInteractions` (new, `src/lib/db.ts`, SQL-layer `tools_used ?| $2::text[]` filter — no `OR intent_bucket = ...` clause, per the asymmetry above) — wired into `runBieCalibration(days = 14)` as a fourth pass alongside 0DTE, SPX Slayer outcomes, and SPX-tool-calling.
- **Wiring, zero new surface needed:** same as task #112 — `GET /api/admin/bie-report` and `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` already render `formatCalibration`'s raw text, so the new section appears automatically.

**Evidence (test run, this PR):**
- New pure-math tests in `bie.test.ts` mirroring task #112's SPX-tool-call test block: cohort inclusion (`tools_used` intersection) / exclusion (generic-only tools); an explicit "intent_bucket alone, without a matching tool, never joins the cohort" test (locks in the tools_used-only asymmetry — guards against a future edit reintroducing an unbacked OR-clause); aggregate math over a mixed cohort (`sum(verified)/sum(total)` = 12/15 = 80%, weighted; avg latency = (4000+6000+40)/3 → 3347ms); claims_total=0 rows counted in `n` but excluded from the grounding ratio; evidence-gating at n=5 (no recommendations), n=10 (gate clears, low-grounding recommendation fires, no router-coverage recommendation), and n=11; empty-cohort → `null` rates; combined 4-section report text and the "no fourth section when unattached" regression.
- New tests in `tool-defs.test.ts`: every `NIGHTHAWK_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` entry is a real `LARGO_TOOL_DEFS` tool, is a subset of `TOOL_GROUPS.platform`, excludes `get_spx_vs_nighthawk_comparison`/`get_platform_snapshot` by name, and has no duplicates.
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: clean.
- Node 22 (`/opt/node22/bin/node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`): **1474/1474 passing, 0 failures.**
- Node 20 (`/opt/node20/bin/node`, same invocation): **1474/1474 passing, 0 failures** — identical count to Node 22, no Node-version-specific gap on this branch.
- `npm run build`: clean. `npm run lint:brand` / `npm run lint:vendor`: clean.
- `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts`: empty — confirmed this PR never touches the live SPX play-engine.

**Fix rationale — why this design, what was deliberately left unchanged:** reused task #112's architecture exactly rather than inventing a new shape, per the task brief — same pure-compute/format/from-db-orchestrator triad, same `MIN_EVIDENCE`, same weighting convention, same fail-open contract. The only intentional divergences from the SPX precedent (tools_used-only cohort test, no `intent_bucket` OR-clause, no router-coverage recommendation, `get_spx_vs_nighthawk_comparison`/`get_platform_snapshot` excluded) are each a direct, documented consequence of Night Hawk's router coverage genuinely being narrower than SPX Slayer's today, not a shortcut — every one is called out inline (`db.ts`, `calibration.ts`, `tool-defs.ts`) specifically so a future reader doesn't mistake reality for a bug and "fix" it by fabricating a match. `spx-signals.ts` and the live play-engine are untouched; this is a fourth, independent, read-only analytics pass over `bie_interactions`.

---

## BIE self-eval: 0DTE Command tool-calling Largo turns get their own calibration cohort — task #149 (branch `fix/zerodte-bie-calibration-cohort`)

**Status:** SHIPPED. Direct analogue of task #112 (SPX Slayer's own tool-calling cohort, already `main`), applied to the OTHER "0DTE"-branded product in this codebase. **Disambiguation, per task #127's standing note:** "0DTE Command" here means the always-on multi-ticker scanner behind `/grid`'s default tab (`src/lib/zerodte/*`, `zerodte_setup_log`/`zerodte_scan_rejections`) — a completely separate engine from SPX Slayer's own single-instrument play engine (`src/lib/spx-signals.ts`, `spx_play_outcomes`), despite both being "0DTE"-flavored branding. This task never touches SPX Slayer's code or tables.

**Root cause / motivation (gap, not a bug):** before this PR, `bie/calibration.ts` had three passes: `computeCalibration` (0DTE Command's own `zerodte_setup_log` — did the SCANNER'S setups make money), `computeSpxCalibration` (SPX Slayer's `spx_play_outcomes`), and task #112's `computeSpxToolCallCalibration` (Largo's answer quality on SPX-Slayer-tool-calling `bie_interactions` turns). None of the three could answer "how good are Largo's ANSWERS specifically when 0DTE Command's own tools (`get_zerodte_plays`, `get_zerodte_rejections`) were involved" — the first pass measures the scanner's own trade P&L, not answer quality, and is a completely different axis. Grounding-verifier claim correctness (`claims_verified`/`claims_total`) says nothing about answer quality on its own: every numeric claim in an answer can be individually grounded/true while the answer still dispatches the wrong tool, misreads which candidates cleared the board's 4 gates, or serves stale board state. This PR closes that continuous-tracking gap for 0DTE Command's own cohort, mirroring task #112's architecture exactly rather than inventing a new one.

**Cohort-membership decision (same UNION reasoning as task #112, verified independently for this product):**
- `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES` (`src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts`, right after `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`) = `["get_zerodte_plays", "get_zerodte_rejections"]` — verified against `run-tool.ts`'s case statements (not guessed from naming): `get_zerodte_plays` → `zeroDtePlaysForLargo()` (`zerodte/scan.ts`) → `readZeroDteLedger()` joined with `scanZeroDteBoard()`'s live finds, reading `zerodte_setup_log`; `get_zerodte_rejections` → `zeroDteRejectionsForLargo()` (`zerodte/rejections.ts`), reading `zerodte_scan_rejections` (task #147). Both read 0DTE Command's OWN persisted/computed state — no generic ticker-scoped tools to exclude here, unlike `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`'s narrowing of `TOOL_GROUPS.spx_desk` (`TOOL_GROUPS.platform`, where both tools live, has no such generic tools bundled in alongside them), so this list is simply the full pair rather than a filtered subset. `tool-defs.test.ts` pins it as a verified subset of `TOOL_GROUPS.platform` plus an exact-pair check and a no-duplicates check, mirroring `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`'s own invariants.
- **Cohort test is a UNION, not a pure `tools_used` check — this is the one product in this task-batch that structurally needed it.** 0DTE Command is the only sibling product (of this wave-4-batch-2 sub-sweep) with a real deterministic router intent: `classifyBieIntent()`'s `ZERODTE_RE` branch (`src/lib/bie/router.ts`) returns `{ intent: "zerodte_plays", ticker: null }` for questions like "how are today's plays doing." The router's `zerodte_plays` answer internally reads the same `zerodte_setup_log` state a Claude-tool-calling turn would read via `get_zerodte_plays`, but `logBie()` always records that router path's `tools_used` as the `["blackout_intelligence"]` sentinel, never the real tool name (same mechanism task #112 documented for SPX's `spx_structure` intent). A pure `tools_used`-intersection test would therefore silently exclude EVERY router-matched 0DTE-board turn from the cohort by construction, making `router_match_rate_pct` read a permanent, meaningless 0% — not because router coverage is actually bad, but purely an artifact of how `tools_used` is populated on that path. Fix: cohort membership = `tools_used ∩ ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES ≠ ∅` **OR** `intent_bucket === "zerodte_plays"` (`isZeroDteToolCallingRow`, `calibration.ts`; SQL-layer equivalent in `fetchZeroDteToolCallingBieInteractions`, `db.ts`).

**What was built, following the exact task #112 architecture (types → cohort predicate → pure compute → format → DB-backed orchestrator → wiring), placed immediately after the task #112 section and clearly marked "Task #149" in `src/lib/bie/calibration.ts`:**
- `ZeroDteToolCallInputRow` / `ZeroDteToolCallCalibrationReport` — same shape as their SPX counterparts (including `intent_bucket`), kept as distinct named types so a caller can never pass one product's rows into the other's compute function.
- `isZeroDteToolCallingRow()` — the UNION predicate above.
- `computeZeroDteToolCallCalibration(rows, window)` — pure, exported, directly unit-tested (no DB mocking): reports `n`, `claude_fallback_n`/`router_matched_n`/`router_match_rate_pct`, `grounding_pass_rate_pct` (aggregate `sum(claims_verified)/sum(claims_total)`, weighted by claim count, over cohort rows that actually carried claims), `avg_latency_ms`, and evidence-gated `recommendations` (same `MIN_EVIDENCE = 10` bar every other pass in this file uses).
- `formatZeroDteToolCallCalibration(r)` — human-readable summary. Deliberately headed **"0DTE Command tool-calling Largo turns"**, not bare "0DTE" — `formatCalibration`'s FIRST section is already headed "0DTE Command calibration" for the completely different scanner-P&L pass, so this section's label makes explicit it is a different axis (Largo answer quality, not scanner outcome) and must never read as a duplicate.
- `CalibrationReport.zerodte_tool_calls: ZeroDteToolCallCalibrationReport | null` — additive field, named distinctly from `spx_tool_calls` so the two products' cohorts can never be confused in code. `computeCalibration()` sets it to `null` alongside the other additive fields; `formatCalibration()` appends a fourth `---`-separated section when present, with a code comment reiterating why this section is not a duplicate of the first. A report without it renders identically to before (regression-tested).
- `fetchZeroDteToolCallingBieInteractions(sinceDate, zeroDteEngineToolNames, limit=3000)` (`src/lib/db.ts`, immediately after `fetchSpxToolCallingBieInteractions`) — copy of that function's SQL shape, including the OR-clause, with the hardcoded intent literal changed from `'spx_structure'` to `'zerodte_plays'`; same SQL-layer cohort filter (Postgres `jsonb ?|` overlap) for the same reason (`bie_interactions` is one row per Largo QUESTION, much higher volume than the admission-gated `zerodte_setup_log` table `fetchZeroDteSetupLogRange` already reads).
- `computeZeroDteToolCallCalibrationFromDb()` (non-exported async orchestrator, same fail-open `try {} catch { return null }` contract as every sibling `*FromDb` helper in this file) wired into `runBieCalibration(days=14)` as a fourth, independent pass alongside the existing three, attaching `report.zerodte_tool_calls`.
- **Wiring, zero new surface needed:** same as task #112 — `GET /api/admin/bie-report` already returns `formatCalibration(calibration)`, and `AdminBieDashboard.tsx`'s calibration panel already renders that text raw, so the new section appears automatically.

**Evidence (test run, this PR):**
- 9 new pure-math tests in `bie.test.ts` mirroring task #112's own test structure: cohort inclusion (`tools_used` intersection with `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`) / exclusion (generic-only tools); a router-matched `zerodte_plays` row joining the cohort despite the `["blackout_intelligence"]` sentinel (and a `spx_structure` router match correctly NOT joining — the cross-product negative case); aggregate math over a mixed cohort (weighted grounding ratio 12/15=80%, avg latency rounding to 3347ms); `claims_total=0` rows counted in `n` but excluded from the grounding ratio; evidence-gating (`n<10` → zero recommendations); recommendations firing once `n≥10`; empty-cohort → `null` rates; a 4-way combined-report text assertion and the "no fourth section when unattached" regression.
- 5 new tests in `tool-defs.test.ts` for `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`: every entry is a real `LARGO_TOOL_DEFS` tool, is a subset of `TOOL_GROUPS.platform`, is exactly the `{get_zerodte_plays, get_zerodte_rejections}` pair, and has no duplicates — mirroring `SPX_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`'s own invariant set (minus the "excludes generic tools" check, which doesn't apply here since there are no generic tools in the pair to exclude).
- `npx tsc --noEmit`: clean.
- Full suite (`node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test $(find src -name '*.test.ts')`): **1473/1473 passing, 0 failures** on both Node 22.22.2 and Node 20.20.2 (`/opt/node20/bin/node`, CI's exact invocation) — identical pass count on both runtimes, no pre-existing failures observed on this branch on either.
- `npm run build`: clean. `npm run lint:brand` / `npm run lint:vendor`: clean.
- `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts`: empty — confirmed before opening this PR, per the task's hard invariant.

**Blast radius:** `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts` (+1 export, `ZERODTE_ENGINE_TOOL_NAMES`, purely additive), `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.test.ts` (+5 tests), `src/lib/db.ts` (+1 function, `fetchZeroDteToolCallingBieInteractions`, nothing existing touched), `src/lib/bie/calibration.ts` (+1 additive `CalibrationReport` field, +1 type pair, +1 predicate, +1 compute fn, +1 format fn, +1 DB orchestrator, +2 lines in `runBieCalibration`/`computeCalibration` — every existing field/function byte-for-byte unchanged), `src/lib/bie/bie.test.ts` (+9 tests). **Concurrency note:** `calibration.ts`, `db.ts`, and `tool-defs.ts` are being edited concurrently by 3 sibling PRs for tasks #133/#137/#144 doing the exact same cohort-calibration pattern for other products — merge conflicts on these three files are expected and will be resolved by a human, not by this PR or its author.

**Fix rationale — why this design, what was deliberately left unchanged:** followed task #112's file layout and naming conventions exactly (type → predicate → compute → format → DB orchestrator → wiring, in that relative order, placed immediately after the task #112 section) rather than inventing a new structure, per this task's explicit instruction to reuse the precedent architecture. Kept `zerodte_tool_calls` and `spx_tool_calls` as clearly-named, separate fields (never merged into one generic "tool_calls" bucket keyed by product) so a future reader can never conflate which product a number belongs to — the exact confusion task #127 already had to fix once for tool descriptions. Left `MIN_EVIDENCE`, the grounding-ratio weighting convention, and the recommendation-threshold philosophy (< 70% grounding, < 30% router-match) matching the file's existing conventions rather than inventing new ones. Deliberately did NOT touch `computeCalibration`'s existing `zerodte_setup_log` pass or its "0DTE Command calibration" header — that pass answers a genuinely different question (scanner P&L) and needed no change; the new section's header was chosen specifically to avoid reading as a duplicate of it.

## 🟡 ADDED 2026-07-05 — Admin dashboard's "SPX health" panel labeled itself "0DTE Command" (branch `fix/admin-spx-health-kicker-mislabel`)

**Status:** SHIPPED (bug fix — cosmetic/labeling only, no data/logic touched). `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty.

**Where:** `src/components/admin/AdminBieDashboard.tsx:601` — the `GlassPanel`'s `kicker` prop on the SPX Slayer health panel (`title="SPX health"`).

**Root cause:** the panel's `kicker` read `"0DTE Command · read-only, never mutates the play engine"` — wrong product name entirely. Confirmed via `git show` across the file's history (`e012462`, task #111's original commit) that this string has been wrong since the panel's very first commit; it was never a merge artifact from this session's later 0DTE Command work (tasks #147/#148/#150), just a copy-paste mistake baked in from day one and carried forward unnoticed through every subsequent PR that touched this file. Everything else about the panel — the surrounding comment block, `admin-spx-health.ts`'s module doc, the metric chips (Phase/Action/Grade/Gates/Desk feed/Flow feed), the recent-signal-log table — is unambiguously about SPX Slayer's own play engine; nothing here is 0DTE-Command-related.

**Evidence:** `git show origin/main:src/components/admin/AdminBieDashboard.tsx | grep -n "kicker=\"0DTE Command\|title=\"SPX health\""` on `main` confirms the bug ships in production today. Found this while reviewing task #138's Thermal-health-panel PR (which sits right next to this one in the file) and cross-checking every panel's kicker string for consistency — `grep -rn "0DTE Command · read-only, never mutates" src/` confirms this was the only occurrence (the genuinely-0DTE-Command panel from task #150 correctly reads `"0DTE Command · read-only, sourced entirely from already-persisted data"`).

**Fix:** one-line string change — `"0DTE Command · read-only, never mutates the play engine"` → `"SPX Slayer · read-only, never mutates the play engine"`.

**Blast radius:** single string, single panel, single file. No other panel, route, or test references this exact string. No behavior change — purely a mislabeled UI kicker that has been silently confusing anyone reading the admin dashboard about which product's health this panel actually reports.

**Fix rationale:** kept the fix in its own branch/PR rather than folding it into task #138's Thermal-panel PR (where I happened to spot it) — unrelated root cause, unrelated file region, and CLAUDE.md's standing policy is one issue per branch/PR.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite **1451/1451 passing** on both Node 22.22.2 and Node 20.20.2 (no test references the old string, so the count is unchanged from baseline). `npm run build` clean. `npm run lint:brand`/`lint:vendor` clean. `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty.

---

## 🧩 ADDED 2026-07-05 — Thermal health panel in AdminBieDashboard (task #138): per-preset-ticker matrix cache freshness + durable regime-transition log + Thermal-cron liveness + GEX-scoped error scan, read-only

**Status:** SHIPPED (`fix/gex-thermal-health-panel`). Internal admin tooling — same scope carve-out as task #111's SPX health panel entry above: `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` is Clerk `role:admin`-gated, not member-facing, and this panel matches the dashboard's existing `GlassPanel`/`MetricChip`/`DataTable` conventions exactly (no new design language).

**Motivation:** every other real-time product now has an admin-visible health glance (SPX Slayer: task #111's SPX health panel; the generic per-cron-job view: `/api/admin/cron-health`) but BlackOut Thermal (the GEX/gamma/dealer-positioning product behind `/heatmap`) had none — an admin had no single place to see "is the shared GEX matrix actually warm for SPY/SPX/etc. right now," "has anything actually crossed today" (task #136's brand-new `gex_regime_events` durable log had zero consumers besides the `get_gex_regime_events` Largo tool until this panel), or "is `gex-alerts`/`heatmap-warm`/`gex-eod-snapshot` really running" without leaving the tab for the generic Crons tab and mentally filtering it down to the 3 Thermal-owned jobs among ~13 registered crons.

**Where:**
- `src/lib/providers/polygon-options-gex.ts` — new `peekGexHeatmapCache(ticker)` + `GexHeatmapCachePeek` type (+78 lines, purely additive, no existing export's signature touched).
- `src/lib/admin-gex-health.ts` (new) — `fetchGexHealthSnapshot()`, the data source; also exports the pure `summarizeRegimeEvents()` helper.
- `src/app/api/admin/gex/health/route.ts` (new) — `GET`, admin-gated (`requireAdminApi()`, identical boilerplate to `/api/admin/spx/health/route.ts`).
- `src/components/admin/AdminBieDashboard.tsx` — new "Thermal health" `GlassPanel` + its own independent fetch/state/effect (`gexHealth`/`loadGexHealth`), wired into the existing "Recompute" `ActionButton` alongside the BIE report's `load()` and SPX health's `loadSpxHealth()`.
- `src/lib/admin-gex-health.test.ts` (new) — 9 cases.
- No `db.ts` schema change (reads task #136's already-shipped `gex_regime_events` table); no new provider/API dependency.

**Design decision — why a new dedicated route, not `/api/admin/bie-report` or a full `admin-spx-dashboard.ts`-style Thermal tool:** `/api/admin/bie-report` is BIE-specific (interactions/calibration/discovery/embeddings probes) — Thermal pipeline health doesn't belong in it, same reasoning task #111's entry gives for SPX. Unlike SPX Slayer, Thermal has no heavier admin dashboard route to reuse or avoid (`admin-spx-dashboard.ts` has no Thermal analogue yet) — this is a from-scratch small glance, deliberately scoped to "is the pipeline alive," not a full GEX-matrix debugging surface.

**Read-only proof (critical constraint: this panel must never trigger an upstream Polygon fetch, a Postgres write, or a cron action just by being viewed):** `peekGexHeatmapCache()` DELIBERATELY does not call `fetchGexHeatmap()` — it checks the in-memory `cachedHeatmaps` mirror first, then a single `sharedCacheGet` Redis read, and reports `cached:false` on a miss rather than building a fresh matrix (a build costs a live Polygon chain fetch: `fetchHeatmapBand` + up to `FAR_DATED_MAX_TARGETS` far-dated fetches). `fetchGexRegimeEvents()` (task #136) is a plain `SELECT` (`fetchGexRegimeEventRows` in `db.ts`) — no write path is imported or called anywhere in `admin-gex-health.ts`. `buildCronHealthSnapshot()` (task-#90-era `admin-cron-health.ts`) is the same read-only snapshot the generic Crons admin tab already calls on every load — filtering its `jobs[]` array to the 3 Thermal keys adds no new read or side effect. `fetchRecentErrorEvents()` (`error-sink.ts`) is a plain `SELECT` against `error_events`. Zero writes anywhere in this leg's call graph — confirmed by inspection, not just by convention (mirrors task #111's line-by-line `mutate:false` trace, adapted to "no write function is even imported" since this pipeline has no mutate-flag to gate).

**What the panel shows:** per-preset-ticker (`heatmapPresetTickers()`, the same ~11-symbol list `heatmap-warm`/`gex-eod-snapshot` already warm/snapshot) cache status — cold/warm/stale, age in seconds, spot at last build, and the LAST cached sample's event count (0 = diffed-and-clean, not "unknown"); a 24h regime-transition summary + the 15 most recent `gex_regime_events` rows (ticker, type, direction, message, detected-at) — covering EVERY watched ticker, not just `gex-alerts`' 3-ticker `REGIME_WATCHLIST` (SPY/SPX/QQQ) push scope; live status/label/age for exactly `heatmap-warm`, `gex-eod-snapshot`, `gex-alerts` (filtered out of the SAME snapshot the generic Crons tab reads — `grid-warm`, a different product's cron, is explicitly excluded, verified by a dedicated test); and up to 10 recent `error_events` rows whose scope/name/message substring-match `/gex|heatmap|thermal/i` — documented in the panel's own comment as a best-effort filter over the generic sink, not a dedicated GEX error-capture path (most chain-fetch failures inside `polygon-options-gex.ts` are `console.warn`-only today and are honestly NOT covered by this leg).

**Resilience:** every one of the four legs inside `fetchGexHealthSnapshot()` is independently try/caught — a single ticker's peek throwing degrades only that ticker's row (not the other ~10), and a regime-events/cron-health/recent-errors failure degrades only that leg, all recorded in `errors: string[]` while the function still returns whatever succeeded (test-covered: 5 of the 9 test cases exercise exactly this "one leg throws, the rest of the snapshot survives" contract). The panel's fetch/state/effect in `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` is fully independent of both the BIE report's and the SPX health panel's own fetches — a Thermal-health failure can never blank either of the other two, and vice versa.

**Blast radius:** three new files, one additive (+78-line) change to `polygon-options-gex.ts` (new export only, no existing function body touched), plus `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` (new types, new state/effect, one new `GlassPanel`, one edit to the existing "Recompute" `ActionButton`'s `onClick` to also call `loadGexHealth()`). No existing exported function's signature changed; no existing route touched; `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (verified before opening this PR, per the standing constraint).

**Fix rationale:** chose a Redis-cache PEEK over calling `fetchGexHeatmap()` directly because the live route's own cache-then-build fallback would make an admin merely opening this tab responsible for real upstream Polygon spend on any ticker that happened to be cold — the exact kind of "observability adds cost" trap task #111's `mutate:false` read-only proof exists to rule out for SPX; here there's no mutate flag to gate, so the equivalent guarantee is structural (the function that could trigger a build is never imported). Chose to filter the EXISTING generic `buildCronHealthSnapshot()` rather than write a second staleness/weekend-multiplier calculator because `admin-cron-health.ts` already encodes real nuance (market-hours-only off-window suppression, the target-fresh override for handshake lag) that would silently drift out of sync with the generic Crons tab if duplicated. Chose a best-effort substring filter for leg 4 over building a dedicated GEX error-capture path because none exists today and inventing one was out of scope for a health-panel task — documented as a coverage gap rather than presented as complete.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite (`node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test`) — **1415/1415 passing** (9 new) on both Node 20.20.2 and Node 22.22.2. `npm run build` clean (`/api/admin/gex/health` present in the route manifest). `npm run lint:brand`/`lint:vendor` clean. `node scripts/verify-api-auth-guards.mjs` — GREEN (123 routes, new route recognized via `requireAdminApi(`). No browser available in this sandbox (Playwright/Chromium blocked) — the rendered UI was **not** visually verified; correctness was checked at the code/type level only, same documented gap as task #111's entry.

## 🧠 ADDED 2026-07-05 — `flows-verifier.ts` validated HELIX's tape aggregates but had ZERO coverage of the flow-ANOMALY detector's own threshold math (task #132, branch `fix/helix-anomaly-detector-correctness-verifier`)

**Status:** SHIPPED (coverage-gap closure in the audit tooling, not a live bug in the app — `detectFlowAnomalies`' actual production behavior was NOT found to be wrong; the *validator* simply had no way to catch it if it ever were). `git diff origin/main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (verified before opening this PR — this task never touches SPX Slayer).

**Where:** `src/app/api/cron/market-regime-detector/flow-anomaly-detection.ts` (`detectFlowAnomalies` gains an additive `opts.rows` injection point), `src/lib/correctness/flow-anomaly-scope.ts` (new file — independent recompute + diff helpers), `src/lib/correctness/flows-verifier.ts` (new imports, module-doc addendum, new `adaptAnomalies`/`adaptNearMisses`/`verifyFlowAnomalyDetector` functions wired into `verifyFlows()`), plus 3 new test files: `flow-anomaly-scope.test.ts`, `flows-verifier.test.ts`, `flow-anomaly-detector-regression.test.ts`.

**Root cause / motivation:** `flows-verifier.ts` (HELIX priority surface #2) validates the raw flow TAPE aggregates served to HELIX members — premium totals, call/put%, recency ordering, and (as of a prior PR) a Massive cross-provider oracle. It has always had zero coverage of the SEPARATE flow-**anomaly** detector that powers the FlowAnomalyBanner (`detectFlowAnomalies`, extracted from `route.ts` in task #131/PR #495, computes `LARGE_PREMIUM_PRINT` ($2M-print) and `DIRECTIONAL_FLOW_SKEW` (10:1-ratio) anomalies + a near-miss band from the SAME per-ticker premium data flows-verifier.ts already reads). A regression in that threshold math / skew-ratio computation / near-miss band — an off-by-one comparison, a flipped `>=`/`>`, a dropped branch, a ratio computed from the wrong side — would have shipped completely silently: nothing short of the banner visibly looking wrong in production would ever have caught it. `grep -rn "detectFlowAnomalies" src/lib/correctness/*.ts` on `main` returns zero matches — the two modules had never been connected.

**Evidence:** Traced `detectFlowAnomalies`'s exact classification loop line-by-line (byTicker grouping via `r.ticker ?? "SPX"`, `callPrem`/`putPrem`/`maxSingle` accumulation, the two threshold branches with their near-miss floors) to write `flow-anomaly-scope.ts`'s `classifyFlowAnomalies` as a faithful, from-scratch re-implementation — own loop, own variables, importing ONLY the 4 exported threshold *constants* (never the classification code itself, so a bug in that loop can't be structurally mirrored in the check meant to catch it). Proved the wiring actually catches a regression with three deliberately-injected mismatches in `flow-anomaly-detector-regression.test.ts` (mocking `detectFlowAnomalies` itself to return a wrong result, while keeping the real threshold constants for the recompute side): (1) silently dropping a real $3M NVDA `LARGE_PREMIUM_PRINT` → flagged, detail names NVDA and "MISSING from the actual output"; (2) fabricating a bogus `GHOST`-ticker anomaly → flagged, detail names GHOST and "UNEXPECTED in the actual output"; (3) reporting the right ticker/type/direction for an AMD skew but a wrong ratio (42 instead of the real ~11.86) → flagged, detail says "value mismatch". Conversely, `flows-verifier.test.ts` proves the REAL (unmocked) `detectFlowAnomalies` agrees cleanly with the independent recompute on realistic NVDA/TSLA/AAPL fixtures (both new metrics `consistency-only`, 0 disagreement) and degrades to `skipped` — never a flag — on a genuinely quiet 30-minute window, matching `detectFlowAnomalies`' own no-op-on-empty-window behavior.

**What was built:**
- `flow-anomaly-scope.ts` (new, pure/side-effect-free — the `flows-verifier.ts` sibling for `gex-odte-scope.ts`'s role in `heatmap-verifier.ts`/`desk-verifier.ts`): `classifyFlowAnomalies(rows)` — from-scratch recompute of `LARGE_PREMIUM_PRINT`/`DIRECTIONAL_FLOW_SKEW` anomalies + the near-miss band (task #131); `diffClassifiedLists(expected, actual)` — a keyed (ticker+type+direction) set-diff with a tiny fp tolerance on `metric_value`, returning `onlyExpected`/`onlyActual`/`valueMismatches`; `classificationDiffIsClean`/`describeClassificationDiff` helpers.
- `detectFlowAnomalies` gains an additive `opts.rows?: FlowRow[]` parameter — when supplied, it skips its own internal `fetchRecentFlows` call and scores the injected rows instead. Purely additive (mirrors task #131's own `opts.nearMisses` precedent exactly): the one pre-existing caller (`route.ts`) omits it and is byte-for-byte behavior-unchanged (confirmed: the full pre-existing `flow-anomaly-detection.test.ts` suite passes unmodified). This exists because the verifier and the live detector would otherwise each issue their OWN independent DB read against a fast-moving 30-minute tape — a print landing between the two reads would look exactly like a detector bug when it is really just a race between two point-in-time queries, which would generate real, periodic false FLAGs during RTH.
- `flows-verifier.ts` wires it together (new `verifyFlowAnomalyDetector` helper, called from `verifyFlows()`): fetches the SAME 30-min/premium-order window `detectFlowAnomalies` itself reads (`fetchRecentFlows({ since_hours: 0.5, order: "premium" })` — a second, separate bounded read from the main 48h/2000-row tape read already above it, since that different window/cap is NOT a faithful stand-in for the 30-min live window), classifies it independently, calls the REAL `detectFlowAnomalies` on the identical injected rows, and diffs the two — producing two new metrics on the existing `"FLOWS"` `TickerScore`: `anomaly_detection` and `anomaly_near_miss` (layer `shadow-recompute`).

**Blast radius:** `flow-anomaly-detection.ts`'s only change is the additive `opts.rows` parameter — the classification loop, thresholds, and near-miss logic are byte-for-byte untouched. `flow-anomaly-near-misses.ts` and `route.ts` (the file's other two consumers) are untouched — neither passes `opts.rows`, so both keep hitting `fetchRecentFlows` internally exactly as before. `run-correctness.ts` needed zero changes — the new checks ride the existing `"FLOWS"` `TickerScore`/`verifyFlows()` call it already has wired in.

**Fix rationale:** chose row-injection over accepting the small TOCTOU race between two independent fetches specifically because a live 30-minute options-flow tape changes fast enough during RTH that the race would produce real, periodic false FLAGs unrelated to any actual detector bug — which would erode trust in the whole auditor (the CLAUDE.md HONESTY PRINCIPLE this verifier suite is built on: consistency-only is a coverage gap, never a false anything). Reused the real exported threshold *constants* (`LARGE_PRINT_THRESHOLD` etc.) rather than hardcoding a second copy of the same numbers in `flow-anomaly-scope.ts` — those are shared POLICY values (route.ts already imports them directly for its own `DEDUP_SUPPRESSED` near-miss path), not logic, and duplicating them would make a deliberate, legitimate threshold change look like a bug here until this file was separately edited too. Deliberately reimplemented the classification LOOP itself from scratch — no import of, or call into, any part of `detectFlowAnomalies` — mirroring `flows-verifier.ts`'s own pre-existing `aggregate()` function, which is "written from scratch... does NOT import flow-service" for the identical independence reason.

**Tests added:**
- `flow-anomaly-scope.test.ts` (17 cases, pure, zero mocks) — `classifyFlowAnomalies` against the exact fixtures `flow-anomaly-detection.test.ts` already established (print anomaly/near-miss/silent, skew anomaly/near-miss/silent-under-volume-gate, the one-sided 99-sentinel ratio, multi-ticker independence, no-rows) plus 7 `diffClassifiedLists` cases (clean match any-order, fp-noise tolerance, missing/extra/value-mismatch/direction-flip mismatches, empty-vs-empty).
- `flows-verifier.test.ts` (3 cases, `mock.module()` on `../db`/`../providers/{config,option-trades,spx-session}`, all RELATIVE paths per this repo's Node 20 `mock.module()`-alias-crash finding) — the real `detectFlowAnomalies` agrees with the recompute for a mixed anomaly+near-miss fixture, a quiet-window skip, and an all-silent fixture.
- `flow-anomaly-detector-regression.test.ts` (3 cases, additionally mocks `flow-anomaly-detection.ts` wholesale — kept in its OWN file since `node --test` isolates `mock.module()` per file, confirmed empirically, so this mock can't leak into `flows-verifier.test.ts`'s real-detector coverage) — the three deliberately-injected mismatch scenarios described in Evidence above, each correctly FLAGGED.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite **1429/1429 passing (23 new)** on both Node 22.22.2 and Node 20.20.2 (`nvm use 20` + the exact glob-expansion invocation `node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test "${files[@]}"`) — identical pass count on both, no Node-20-only regressions. `npm run build` clean. `npm run lint:brand`/`lint:vendor` clean. `git diff origin/main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty.

## 🧩 ADDED 2026-07-05 — 0DTE Command admin health panel: last-scan-time / candidates-scanned / rejection-rate (task #150, branch `fix/zerodte-command-admin-health-panel`)

**Status:** SHIPPED (enhancement, not a bug fix — closes an observability gap). Internal admin tooling — `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` is an already-existing admin dashboard gated behind Clerk `role:admin`, not a member-facing page, so this is in scope directly (matches task #111's own scoping note verbatim: no new design language invented, this panel reuses that dashboard's existing `GlassPanel`/`MetricChip` conventions exactly). `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty (verified before opening this PR — this task never touches SPX Slayer).

**Motivation:** SPX Slayer got an admin health panel in task #111 (`AdminBieDashboard.tsx`'s "SPX health" `GlassPanel`); 0DTE Command — the SEPARATE multi-ticker scanner branded "0DTE Command" in-app (`/grid`'s default tab, formerly "BlackOut Grid"; see task #127's entry below for the naming confusion this distinction already had to be fixed once) — had no equivalent. An admin glancing at BIE had no way to see "is the 0DTE Command scan pipeline actually running, and how much of the tape is it throwing away" without reading raw Postgres rows.

**Where:**
- `src/lib/admin-zerodte-health.ts` (new) — `fetchZeroDteHealthSnapshot()`, the data source.
- `src/app/api/admin/zerodte/health/route.ts` (new) — `GET`, admin-gated (`requireAdminApi()`, same boilerplate as `/api/admin/spx/health/route.ts`).
- `src/components/admin/AdminBieDashboard.tsx` — new "0DTE Command health" `GlassPanel` + its own independent fetch/state/effect (`zeroDteHealth`/`loadZeroDteHealth`), wired into the existing "Recompute" button alongside the BIE report's and SPX health panel's own `load()`/`loadSpxHealth()`.
- `src/lib/admin-zerodte-health.test.ts` (new) — 12 cases.
- No `db.ts` schema change; no new provider/API dependency; no new instrumentation/counters — every figure is read from tables/functions that already existed before this task (confirmed first, per the task's explicit instruction to check before adding anything new).

**Where each of the 3 required metrics is actually sourced from (read in full before designing, per instruction):**
1. **last-scan-time** — there is **no dedicated `zerodte` entry in `cron-registry.ts`**. Traced the real call graph: `warmZeroDteBoard()` (`src/lib/zerodte/scan.ts`) is the only path that ever calls `persistZeroDteScan()`/`persistZeroDteRejections()`, and it is invoked exclusively from `src/app/api/cron/grid-warm/route.ts`'s `GET`, inside the same `Promise.allSettled` as 8 unrelated market-wide Grid warmers, logged via `logCronRun("grid-warm", ...)`. Per the task's explicit instruction to "mirror exactly" an existing `logCronRun`-based mechanism rather than invent a new one, this panel calls `buildCronHealthSnapshot()` (`admin-cron-health.ts`) — the exact same function `AdminCronDashboard.tsx`, `/api/admin/bie-report`, and the `cron-staleness-watchdog` cron already call — and reads its `grid-warm` job entry verbatim (`status`/`status_label`/`age_min`/`stale_after_min` all come from that function's own market-hours-aware staleness logic; none of it is reimplemented here). **Documented limitation, not silently glossed over:** a "healthy" grid-warm tick proves the CRON ran, not that the 0DTE Command scan specifically found or logged anything that tick — `grid-warm`'s own `logCronRun` payload has no per-warmer breakdown (`{ ok, warmed, failed, total }` only). Adding one would be new instrumentation the task said to avoid unless nothing existing already captures this — and something does (the cron-run history itself), just at grid-warm's granularity, not 0DTE-Command-only granularity.
2. **candidates-scanned** — confirmed via `deriveZeroDteSetups()` (`board.ts`) that every aggregated candidate ticker in a scan cycle ends up in EXACTLY one of two places: `setups[]` (cleared all 4 gates) or, when `opts.rejections` is supplied, a `ZeroDteGateRejection` push (failed a gate) — there is no third, silently-dropped outcome. But `zerodte_scan_rejections` (task #147) is **throttled to one row per (ticker, gate_failed, direction) state-transition**, not one row per scan cycle (`rejections.ts`'s own module doc) — so the persisted table cannot reconstruct "how many candidates were considered in the LAST scan tick," only "how many distinct tickers have transitioned through a rejected state at some point today." This panel therefore reports **today-session-cumulative distinct tickers**, not a last-cycle count: `committed_count` (distinct tickers in `fetchZeroDteSetupLog(today)`) + `rejected_count` (distinct tickers in `fetchZeroDteRejections()` filtered to today's `session_date`, MINUS any ticker also in `committed_count`). The subtraction matters: a ticker that failed a gate earlier today but later cleared every gate and made the board is a genuine success by end of day, not a rejection — without it, that ticker would be double-counted across both tables.
3. **rejection-rate** — `rejected_count / candidates_scanned`, `null` (never a fabricated `0`) when `candidates_scanned` is `0` (nothing scanned yet today — an honest "no data" state, same discipline this doc's other near-miss-log entries (#108, #147, #131) apply to unevaluated-gate fields).

**Sampling caveat, surfaced rather than silently under-reported:** `fetchZeroDteRejections()` (task #147's read path) has no `session_date` filter param — this panel fetches its most-recent 500 rows (`REJECTIONS_SAMPLE_LIMIT`, generous vs. the throttled write volume rejections.ts documents: single digits to low tens of distinct tickers/day) and filters to today in JS, rather than widening `fetchZeroDteScanRejections`'s SQL signature for a read this narrow in scope. `rejections_sample_capped` is `true` only when the full 500-row page came back AND its oldest row is still from today (proving there could be earlier-today rows beyond the page); it's `false` whenever the page's oldest row already rolled off to a prior day (proving every one of today's rows was necessarily captured) or the page came back under the limit at all. Surfaced in the panel exactly like `data-validator.mjs`'s own `INFO`/"skipped" idiom for a check that couldn't fully run.

**Resilience:** every external call inside `fetchZeroDteHealthSnapshot()` is individually try/caught (`buildCronHealthSnapshot()`, `fetchZeroDteSetupLog()`, `fetchZeroDteRejections()` independently) — a failure in any one leg degrades only that leg (`errors: string[]` records what broke) and still returns a 200 with whatever else succeeded, same contract as `admin-spx-health.ts`. The panel's own fetch/state/effect in `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` is fully independent of the main `/api/admin/bie-report` fetch AND the SPX health panel's own fetch — a failure fetching 0DTE Command health can never blank or block either.

**Blast radius:** three new files, plus `AdminBieDashboard.tsx` (new type, new state/effect, one new `GlassPanel`, one edit to the existing "Recompute" `ActionButton`'s `onClick` to also call `loadZeroDteHealth()`). No existing exported function's signature changed (`fetchZeroDteSetupLog`, `fetchZeroDteRejections`, `buildCronHealthSnapshot`, `todayEt` are all called exactly as already exported); no existing route touched; `zerodte_setup_log`/`zerodte_scan_rejections`'s own writers (`persistZeroDteScan`/`persistZeroDteRejections`) are completely untouched — this panel is read-only, additive, and one more reader alongside the existing Largo tools (`get_zerodte_plays`/`get_zerodte_rejections`) that already read the same two tables.

**Fix rationale — what was deliberately left unchanged:** did not add a dedicated `zerodte` key to `cron-registry.ts` — that cron literally does not exist as a separate HTTP route (`warmZeroDteBoard()` is a function call inside `grid-warm`'s handler, not its own endpoint), so registering a phantom cron key with no route to fire it would misrepresent the system, not clarify it. Did not widen `fetchZeroDteScanRejections`'s SQL to accept a `session_date` param — the existing `{ ticker, limit }` signature is reused by the Largo tool (`get_zerodte_rejections`) and this panel with zero changes; a 500-row page filtered in JS is simpler and sufficient for a health-glance panel, and avoids touching a function 2 other call sites already depend on for an admin-only, non-hot-path read. Did not call `scanZeroDteBoard()` live from the admin route to get an exact "this instant" candidate count — the task's own guidance was to derive these numbers from already-persisted data, and a live call would also trigger the same HELIX-tape/Night-Hawk-dossier pipeline the ~2-min cron already runs, on every admin page load, for no benefit over what the persisted tables already answer session-cumulatively.

**Tests added:** `src/lib/admin-zerodte-health.test.ts` (new, 12 cases, `mock.module("./db", …)` / `mock.module("./zerodte/rejections", …)` / `mock.module("./admin-cron-health", …)` / `mock.module("./nighthawk/session", …)` — all RELATIVE paths, per this repo's documented Node 20 `mock.module()`-alias-crash gotcha) — happy path (distinct committed + rejected tickers combine correctly); scan summary sourced verbatim from `buildCronHealthSnapshot`'s `grid-warm` entry; the committed-later-after-being-rejected-earlier dedup case (counts once, as committed); a rejection from a PRIOR `session_date` excluded from today's counts; zero candidates today → `rejection_rate: null`, never a fabricated `0`; cron-health/setup-log/rejections-log failures each independently degrade without throwing and without blanking the other legs; both `rejections_sample_capped` boundary cases (full page with oldest-row-still-today → `true`; full page with oldest-row-rolled-off → `false`) plus the under-limit case (`false`); `db_configured` mirrors `dbConfigured()` verbatim.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite passing on both Node 22.22.2 and Node 20.20.2 (`nvm use 20` + CI's exact glob-expansion invocation `node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test "${files[@]}"`) — see PR for exact before/after counts. `npm run build` clean (`/api/admin/zerodte/health` present in the route manifest). `npm run lint:brand`/`lint:vendor` clean. `node scripts/verify-api-auth-guards.mjs` GREEN (new route added, admin-gated). No browser available in this sandbox (Playwright/Chromium blocked) — the rendered UI was **not** visually verified; correctness was checked at the code/type level only (`tsc` catches JSX/prop-type errors even without rendering, and `next build` statically renders/prerenders during build). `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty.

## 🟡 FIXED 2026-07-05 — `get_nighthawk_edition` vs `get_platform_snapshot`: thin, non-disambiguating descriptions risked Largo answering a full-detail or dated Night Hawk question with a headline-only, latest-only summary; NIGHTHAWK_RE had no "edition" token at all (task #143, branch `fix/largo-nighthawk-snapshot-disambiguation`)

**Where:** `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts` (`get_nighthawk_edition` ~line 227, `get_platform_snapshot` ~line 236), `src/lib/largo/intent-keywords.ts` (`NIGHTHAWK_RE`, new `NIGHTHAWK_DATED_EDITION_RE`), `src/lib/largo/question-intent.ts` (`analyzeLargoQuestion`, `LargoQuestionIntent` — new `needsNighthawkDatedEdition`), `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.test.ts`, `src/lib/largo/question-intent.test.ts`.

**Root cause (description quality):** both tools can plausibly answer a Night-Hawk-flavored question, and the pre-fix descriptions gave Claude no way to tell them apart:
1. `get_nighthawk_edition`'s description was a terse one-liner ("Night Hawk evening playbook — top plays, recap, market context. Same data as /nighthawk.") that never stated its actual return shape or that its `date` param is the ONLY way to pull a specific past edition.
2. `get_platform_snapshot`'s description ("Cross-service snapshot: SPX desk + flow tape + Night Hawk edition in one call.") literally uses the phrase "Night Hawk edition," implying it hands back the same full edition object `get_nighthawk_edition` does. It does not, by default: read `getPlatformSnapshot()` (`src/lib/platform/index.ts`) — its `nighthawk` field is always `nighthawk.summarizeNightHawkEdition(...)`/`getLatestNightHawkSummary()`, a `NightHawkEditionSummary` (`src/lib/platform/types.ts`) stripped down to `available`/`edition_for`/`published_at`/`recap_headline`/`play_count`/`top_tickers` — `top_tickers` is just `edition.plays.slice(0, 5).map(p => p.ticker)`, bare ticker strings with **no** thesis, entry_range, target, stop, options_play, score, or conviction at all. The full `PlaybookPlay[]` array only appears under a separate `nighthawk_edition` field, and only when the caller explicitly passes `full_edition: true` — and even then, `getPlatformSnapshot()` always calls `nighthawk.getLatestNightHawkEdition()`, never `getNightHawkEditionForDate()`, so it can **never** serve a specific past date regardless of any flag. A question like "what were Night Hawk's entries and targets last night" or "what did Night Hawk pick on July 2nd" routed to `get_platform_snapshot` (its description literally advertises "Night Hawk edition") would silently return either a headline-only summary (no `full_edition`) or the wrong night's picks entirely (any `date`, since `full_edition` is always latest-only) — the same class of "description claim doesn't match the real returned shape" bug task #130 found for `get_options_flow`'s stale "UW ONLY" claim, just one level more dangerous here because the wrong answer isn't just thinner, it can be for the wrong day.

**Root cause (routing regex, task's ask #4):** investigated `NIGHTHAWK_RE` for a task #110/#127/#130-style gap, and found one: `NIGHTHAWK_RE = /\b(nighthawk|night hawk|playbook|tomorrow|evening plays|hawk plays|top plays|hawk)\b/` had **no "edition"/"editions" token at all**, even though the live `/nighthawk` UI itself (`PlaybookBoard.tsx`) renders "Edition live" and "Prior edition" as its own primary vocabulary — a member asking "is a new edition live yet" or "what's in tonight's edition" uses completely natural, in-product language that matched none of the regex's existing alternatives. Verified via a standalone `getToolsForIntent()` repro (see Evidence): that phrasing got `needsNightHawk`-equivalent `false`, `TOOL_GROUPS.platform` (`get_nighthawk_edition`, `get_platform_snapshot`, `get_nighthawk_outcomes`, `get_nighthawk_dossier`, `get_spx_vs_nighthawk_comparison`, the BIE tools, `get_flow_anomaly_near_misses`) was never added to that turn's tool allowlist, and `getToolsForIntent`'s own `names.size <= 2` fallback dumped the **entire unrelated `CORE_TOOLS` bundle** (SPX desk + stock analysis + vol analysis — 40+ tools) instead. Exactly the class of bug task #130 found and fixed for `FLOW_RE`'s missing "flows"/"flowing" siblings, just with a completely absent token rather than a missing inflection. Mitigating factor, checked and worth stating precisely: `largo-live-feed.ts` line 141 unconditionally pre-fetches `get_nighthawk_edition` (latest edition) into every turn's live feed regardless of intent, so the *latest-edition* case is not actually blind — Largo already has that data in context even when the explicit tool call is unreachable. What the regex gap actually breaks is any **explicit tool call** for this data (e.g. a `date`-scoped lookup or a cross-check via `get_platform_snapshot`), since the always-on prefetch only ever calls `get_nighthawk_edition()` with no `date` argument.

**Evidence:** confirmed the literal pre-fix description strings via `git show origin/main:src/lib/largo/tool-defs.ts`. Confirmed the regex gap and its allowlist consequence with a standalone repro calling `getToolsForIntent`/`analyzeLargoQuestion` directly (both pure functions of the question string, no mocking needed):
```
getToolsForIntent("what's in tonight's edition") // pre-fix
// → ['get_market_context', ...TOOL_GROUPS.spx_desk, ...TOOL_GROUPS.stock_analysis, ...TOOL_GROUPS.vol_analysis]
// get_nighthawk_edition / get_platform_snapshot: ABSENT
```
Traced `getPlatformSnapshot()` (`src/lib/platform/index.ts`) and `summarizeNightHawkEdition()`/`getLatestNightHawkSummary()` (`src/lib/platform/nighthawk-service.ts`) line-by-line to confirm the default-slim/latest-only claims, and confirmed `get_spx_structure`'s run-tool.ts case (`marketPlatform.spx.getSpxDeskSummary()`) and `get_flow_tape`'s case (`marketPlatform.flows.getFlowTapeSummary()`) call the **exact same functions** `getPlatformSnapshot()`'s `spx`/`flows` jobs do — so the new description's "exact same object" claims for those two fields are verified, not approximated. Also confirmed `PlatformServiceId = "spx" | "flows" | "nighthawk" | "largo"` (`src/lib/platform/types.ts`) and `get_platform_snapshot`'s own `include` schema enum both list `"largo"`, but `getPlatformSnapshot()`'s job list only branches on `spx`/`flows`/`nighthawk` — `"largo"` is a real, verified no-op in the tool's own parameter surface (not fixed here — a behavior change to `getPlatformSnapshot()` is out of this task's description/routing scope — but now documented in the tool's own description rather than silently doing nothing).

**Fix:**
1. **Rewrote both descriptions** to the `get_ecosystem_context`/`get_spx_play` depth (task #110/#123/#130 precedent). `get_nighthawk_edition` now states its exact full return shape (every `PlaybookPlay` field, every edition-level state flag) and that `date` is the *only* way any Night Hawk tool can serve a specific past edition. `get_platform_snapshot` now states, per field, exactly what it returns and where it comes from — `spx`/`flows` as "the exact same object get_spx_structure/get_flow_tape returns," `nighthawk` as a stripped summary by default with the precise fields that are and aren't present, and `full_edition: true`'s scope explicitly capped at "always the LATEST published edition — no date parameter here." Each description names the other tool by name for direct disambiguation, and `get_platform_snapshot`'s description (plus its `include` schema's own `description` string) now says `'largo'` is currently a no-op instead of silently implying it does something.
2. **Widened `NIGHTHAWK_RE`** to add `edition`/`editions` as explicit bare alternatives — matching this regex's own pre-existing flat-alternation style (not a new co-occurrence idiom), the same "minimal, precedent-matching" choice task #130 made for `FLOW_RE`'s `flows`/`flowing` fix.
3. **Added `NIGHTHAWK_DATED_EDITION_RE`** (`intent-keywords.ts`) + `needsNighthawkDatedEdition` (`question-intent.ts`) — fires only when a nighthawk-ish token (`nighthawk|night hawk|hawk|playbook|edition|editions`) co-occurs with a date/day qualifier (`yesterday|last night|prior night|previous night|night before|last week|<weekday name>|<YYYY-MM-DD>|<M/D[/YY]>`), same "REQUIRE co-occurrence, never fire on a bare generic word alone" discipline as `ZERODTE_REJECTION_RE`/`FLOW_ANOMALY_NEAR_MISS_RE` (a bare "yesterday" or "last Monday" alone is common phrasing for a stock's prior close or an earnings date, and must not fire on its own). Unlike the generic `needsNightHawk` pair (which hints `get_nighthawk_edition` AND `get_platform_snapshot` with zero preference), this pushes `get_nighthawk_edition` specifically **and** a dedicated guidance line ("call get_nighthawk_edition with `date` set... get_platform_snapshot's nighthawk data is always the latest edition only... and can never answer a specific past date") — reusing the exact same "extra descriptive guidance string, not just a tool name" mechanism `needsFlow`'s strike_stacks reminder already established in this file, since a missing `date` argument is a parameter-level mistake a tool-name hint alone wouldn't prevent.

**Also checked, no fix needed:** `get_nighthawk_dossier` (task #129/#488) shares the word "edition" in its own `date` param description ("Edition date YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to latest") but its surface vocabulary (scored/excluded, sub-scores, `fundamental_block`, `trading_halt`) is about a ticker's research grade behind a pick, not the published plays themselves — no overlap risk with `get_nighthawk_edition`/`get_platform_snapshot` found. `get_nighthawk_outcomes` (track record/win-loss) and `get_spx_vs_nighthawk_comparison` (head-to-head vs SPX Slayer) are similarly distinct in vocabulary; left untouched.

**Blast radius:** `tool-defs.ts` (2 description strings + `include`/`full_edition` schema description text — no `run-tool.ts` change, no data any tool returns changed; `git diff main -- src/lib/largo/run-tool.ts` is empty). `intent-keywords.ts` (`NIGHTHAWK_RE` widened — every existing alternative untouched, so every pre-existing match keeps matching; one new regex added, nothing removed). `question-intent.ts` (`LargoQuestionIntent` gains one new boolean field, additive; `guidance` gains one new conditional line, only present when `needsNighthawkDatedEdition` fires). The `NIGHTHAWK_RE` widening is read by both `question-intent.ts` (soft guidance hints) and `tool-defs.ts`'s `getToolsForIntent` (the hard tool allowlist) — both now correctly react to "edition"/"editions" phrasing. No effect on `largo-live-feed.ts`'s unconditional `get_nighthawk_edition` prefetch (untouched, still latest-only by design). `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` is **empty** — confirmed before opening this PR; no SPX Slayer scoring/gating logic touched.

**Fix rationale:** kept the `NIGHTHAWK_RE` fix to the same minimal, precedent-matching shape task #130 used for `FLOW_RE` (add the missing token as a flat alternative, no structural rewrite) rather than converting the whole regex to a co-occurrence pattern — that would be a much larger, riskier change to a regex several other files already depend on for its current (intentionally broad, no-co-occurrence) behavior. Reserved the NEW co-occurrence-gated regex (`NIGHTHAWK_DATED_EDITION_RE`) for the one case that is a genuine **capability** gap, not just a wording-quality one: only `get_nighthawk_edition`'s `date` param can ever answer a dated question, so that's where an extra, more specific hint plus a parameter-level guidance line earns its keep; a "prefer `get_platform_snapshot` for cross-service wording" regex was considered and deliberately left out, since (a) the hint system here is additive-only, never preferential — `get_platform_snapshot` is already hinted on every generic `needsNightHawk` match, so a second regex pushing the same tool name again would be a no-op — and (b) the parameter this case actually needs (`full_edition: true`) is a normal default-off flag, not a missing capability, so the tool's own rewritten description is the right, sufficient lever for it (per this task's own framing: regex/hint work is for "the model reaches for the wrong TOOL entirely," description work is for "right tool, wrong parameter default"). Documented the `'largo'` include no-op in-place (both the tool description and its own schema field) rather than fixing `getPlatformSnapshot()`'s job-dispatch code, since implementing real "largo" data or removing the enum value is a `run-tool.ts`/`platform/index.ts` behavior change outside this task's explicit description/routing scope — flagged here for whoever picks up a `PlatformServiceId` cleanup next.

**Tests added:**
- `src/lib/largo/tool-defs.test.ts` (+6 cases): `get_nighthawk_edition` documents its full play-level fields (thesis/entry_range/target/stop/score) and states its `date` param is uniquely its own; `get_platform_snapshot` documents its default stripped summary, `full_edition`'s latest-only scope, and the missing date parameter; both descriptions agree on the same "what's missing from the summary" vocabulary; `get_platform_snapshot` documents its `spx`/`flows` fields as the exact same objects `get_spx_structure`/`get_flow_tape` return; `get_platform_snapshot` documents the `'largo'` no-op; a `getToolsForIntent` regression test proving bare "edition" wording now puts both tools on the allowlist (the concrete regex-gap fix).
- `src/lib/largo/question-intent.test.ts` (+4 cases): dated Night Hawk wording (yesterday/last night/weekday/explicit date, each paired with a nighthawk-ish token) sets `needsNighthawkDatedEdition` and adds the dedicated guidance line; bare Night Hawk wording with no date qualifier does NOT set it (stays on the pre-existing, unpreferenced pair); bare date/day wording with NO Night Hawk token does NOT falsely fire it (false-positive discipline, mirroring `ZERODTE_REJECTION_RE`/`FLOW_ANOMALY_NEAR_MISS_RE`'s own regression tests); "edition"/"editions" phrasing triggers the `get_nighthawk_edition`/`get_platform_snapshot` guidance hints (the `NIGHTHAWK_RE` token-gap fix, from the `analyzeLargoQuestion` side).

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Full suite **1428/1428 passing on both Node 22.22.2 and Node 20.20.2** (`nvm use 20`; CI's exact `node --import tsx --experimental-test-module-mocks --test "${files[@]}"` invocation on both, re-run after rebasing onto main's task #150/#136 landings) — identical pass count on both, no Node-20-vs-22 divergence; 10 new tests (6 in `tool-defs.test.ts`, 4 in `question-intent.test.ts`). `npm run build` clean. `npm run lint:brand`/`npm run lint:vendor` clean. `git diff main -- src/lib/spx-signals.ts` empty.
